We introduce the notion of morphic accumulators, which replaces the DI hash with a discrete logarithm encoding $H_g(x) = g^x$, mapping set elements to a group before accumulation. We prove, under a variant of the subset product assumption in the generic group model, that this encoding is inherently division intractable, achieving the same security guarantee as random-oracle DI hashes, while simultaneously being a group homomorphism: accumulated elements retain their group-algebraic relationships. This resolves a fundamental tension between compact representation and algebraic structure: the accumulator serves simultaneously as a binding commitment to a set and as a substrate for homomorphic computation over its elements.

Morphic accumulators yield asymptotically optimal constructions across multiple domains: range proofs with $O(n)$ prover time, $O(1)$ proof size, $O(1)$ verification with transparent setups (the first scheme to simultaneously achieve these optimal bounds); polynomial commitments with $O(n)$ prover and $O(1)$ proof size, resolving the cubic bottleneck in prior constant-proof-size GUO-based schemes; and the first linkable ring signatures with $O(1)$ signature size, transparent setup, $O(n)$ offline signing and $O(1)$ online signing.

item.title: Silentium revisited: Pseudorandom Beaver Triple Expansion
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1201

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在安全多方计算（MPC）中，SPDZ类协议依赖Beaver三元组（Beaver triples）实现高效乘法运算，而其生成是整个协议中最耗资源的环节。Silentium（Rieder, PrivCryp’25）是Boyle等人提出的伪随机相关生成器（PCG）的首个实用实现，专为Beaver三元组设计，显著降低通信开销（较MP-SPDZ减少一个数量级），但存在三大实践瓶颈：仅支持素域$\mathbb{F}_p$、缺乏高效预处理扩展机制、原始协议描述存在技术性缺陷。

主要贡献

本研究对Silentium进行系统性重构与增强，提出三项理论与工程并重的创新：
1. 二元域三元组生成：首次将PCG拓展至二元域$\mathbb{F}_{2^\lambda}$，提出适配该域的数论变换（NTT）实例化方案，兼顾计算效率与安全性，突破原有素域限制，为硬件友好型MPC（如FPGA/ASIC部署）提供基础支持；
2. Beaver三元组扩展机制：设计首个基于二元域三元组的三元组扩展方案，类比OT扩展范式，可将少量“种子”三元组高效扩展为海量三元组，显著加速预处理阶段，彻底解决Silentium原生缺乏可扩展预处理的实践短板；
3. 实现级修正与澄清：定位并修复原始协议中影响正确性的关键技术漏洞（涉及多项式插值与共享验证逻辑），提供完整、可复现的实现细节，确保理论协议与实际代码严格一致。

意义

本工作不仅提升了PCG在二元域下的实用性与通用性，更通过扩展机制将预处理成本降至亚线性，为低延迟、高吞吐MPC系统（如实时隐私保护机器学习）提供了坚实支撑。

AI Summary (English)

This paper revisits Silentium—the first practical implementation of a Pseudorandom Correlation Generator (PCG) for Beaver triples—and makes three key contributions. First, we extend PCG to binary fields $\mathbb{F}_{2^\lambda}$, proposing a tailored Number Theoretic Transform (NTT) instantiation that enables efficient and secure triple generation—overcoming Silentium’s original restriction to prime fields. Second, we introduce a Beaver triple expansion scheme, an oblivious-transfer-style protocol that amplifies a small seed batch of binary triples into a large batch, enabling highly efficient preprocessing and resolving Silentium’s practical scalability gap. Third, we identify and fix a critical technical bug in Silentium’s original theoretical description—specifically in polynomial interpolation and consistency verification—thereby clarifying and solidifying its implementation blueprint. Our enhancements preserve Silentium’s low-communication advantage while broadening applicability, improving efficiency, and ensuring correctness.

Abstract

In the line of the SPDZ protocol for secure multi-party computation,
the generation of Beaver triples is the most expensive task.
Silentium (Rieder, PrivCryp 25) is the implementation of a Pseudorandom
Correlation Generator (PCG) for Beaver triples (Boyle et al.,
Crypto 20). PCGs focus on low-communication costs., e.g. their PCG reduces
the communication by one order of magnitude compared to protocols
in MP-SPDZ. Silentium is an implementation of their PCG, achieving
similar running times than MP-SPDZ. We make three theoretical
contributions to Silentium, including an implementation. First, we make
a practical proposal how to generate Beaver triples over binary fields F2λ,
which extends the previous setting over prime fields. For this, we propose
a suitable instantiation of the Number Theoretic Transform. Second, we
show how to use the binary triples to construct what we call a Beaver
triple expansion scheme, that is we construct a scheme that expands a
small batch of Beaver triples into a large batch of Beaver triples, in the
sense of recently established oblivious transfer extension schemes. This
feature enables an efficient preprocessing stage for the PCG, closing a
practical issue of Silentium. Finally, we provide details about the Silentium
implementation, by clearing a technical bug in the initial theoretical
protocol description.

item.title: Character Block Encodings for Discrete CKKS: Single-Level LUTs and Low-Depth Arithmetic
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1200

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

传统离散CKKS方案依赖功能自举（functional bootstrapping） 实现查找表（LUT）计算与模约简，但该流程将LUT评估、模约简、噪声清理与密文刷新强行耦合为单一刚性管道。导致任意大小为 $t$ 的字母表上的LUT需 $\mathcal{O}(\log_2 t)$ 乘法深度，且每次调用均大幅消耗模数预算，严重制约效率与可扩展性。

创新方法：字符块编码（Character Block Encodings）

本文提出不优化自举本身，而重构数据表示的根本性思路——引入块编码（block encodings）：将有限字母表值映射至多个CKKS槽位，其坐标构成该字母表上的函数基（如有限阿贝尔群的特征标）。在此基下，任意LUT均为仿射明文映射，可在单层乘法内完成（深度与 $t$ 无关）；模 $t$ 运算天然成立（块编码仅能表示模 $t$ 剩余类）；且因编码值位于单位圆上，噪声增长独立于 $t$——最坏情形媲美二元域 $\mathbb{Z}_2$ 的标准离散CKKS，典型场景下噪声线性增长，相较传统方案在所有 $t>2$ 时实现指数级改进。

关键成果

  • 噪声清理退化为常数深度（≤4层） 过程（仅含固定三次Smoothstep非线性）；
  • 密文刷新回归经典维护角色，无需绑定LUT，兼容任意CKKS自举实现；
  • 实例化多种块编码：支持模加/模乘/XOR/Min-Max等单次CKKS乘法即可完成；
  • 构建高效CRT大模数算术及有限状态前缀扫描：$d$ 位进制加/减深度为 $4 + \lceil\log_2 d\rceil$，等值/比较深度为 $3 + \lceil\log_2 d\rceil$；
  • 实验验证：256位CRT模加/乘仅需1层乘法深度，单线程延迟低至4.7 ms。

AI Summary (English)

Functional bootstrapping in discrete CKKS couples LUT evaluation, modular reduction, noise cleaning, and ciphertext refreshing into a rigid pipeline—causing multiplicative depth $\mathcal{O}(\log_2 t)$ and heavy modulus budget consumption for alphabet size $t$. We decouple these tasks via character block encodings: representing values as vectors in a function basis (e.g., characters of a finite abelian group) across multiple CKKS slots. Under this representation, any LUT becomes an affine plaintext map evaluable in single multiplicative level, modular arithmetic is native, and noise growth is independent of $t$—matching $\mathbb{Z}_2$ worst-case and scaling linearly (vs. exponential in standard discrete CKKS). Noise cleaning reduces to ≤4-level constant-depth computation, and refreshing reverts to optional depth recovery—enabling use of any CKKS bootstrapping. Instantiations enable modular addition/multiplication, XOR, and min/max with one CKKS multiplication; CRT arithmetic and prefix scans achieve depth $4 + \lceil \log_2 d \rceil$ (add/sub) and $3 + \lceil \log_2 d \rceil$ (equality/comparison). A 256-bit CRT modular operation completes in one multiplication level and 4.7 ms (single thread).

Abstract

Functional bootstrapping has made discrete computation practical in the Cheon-Kim-Kim-Song (CKKS) scheme, but it fuses four distinct tasks - lookup table (LUT) evaluation, modular reduction, noise cleaning, and ciphertext refreshing - into a single rigid pipeline. As a consequence, a generic LUT over an alphabet of size $t$ costs multiplicative depth proportional to $\log_2 t$ and consumes a large share of the modulus budget during a fixed bootstrapping procedure, invoked each time a LUT evaluation or modular reduction is needed.
We show that this pipeline can be unbundled by changing the representation, rather than optimizing the bootstrapping, through block encodings. A finite-alphabet value is carried across several CKKS slots whose coordinates form a basis of functions on the alphabet, typically the characters of a finite abelian group. In such a basis, every LUT is an affine plaintext map evaluated in a single multiplicative level, with depth independent of $t$. Modular reduction comes for free: a block encoding cannot represent anything but a residue, so arithmetic modulo $t$ is native. Because the encoded values lie on the unit circle, noise growth is independent of the alphabet size $t$. In the worst case, it matches the noise growth of standard discrete CKKS on the smallest alphabet $\mathbb Z_2$, and in more typical workloads it is linear in the number of operations, exponentially better than discrete CKKS at every $t > 2$. Noise cleaning becomes a constant-depth procedure of at most four levels, because the alphabet-dependent part is an LUT and only a fixed-degree-3 smoothstep is nonlinear. Finally, since LUTs are no longer part of the bootstrapping, refreshing reverts to its classical role as a maintenance operation invoked only to regain multiplicative depth. Any CKKS bootstrapping can be used, rather than a constrained and expensive pipeline.
We instantiate the framework with several block encodings that make modular addition, modular multiplication, xor or min/max possible with a single CKKS multiplication. We use them to build CRT arithmetic over large composite moduli, and finite-state prefix scans for radix addition and subtraction in depth $4 + \lceil\log_2 d\rceil$ and for equality and comparison in depth $3 + \lceil\log_2 d\rceil$ for $d$ radix digits. For example, a 256-bit CRT modular addition or multiplication consumes a single multiplicative level and has a latency of 4.7 ms on a single thread.

item.title: A Post-Quantum Commitment Scheme from Richelot Isogeny Walks on Superspecial Genus-2 Jacobians
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1199

AI Summary (中文)

基于Richelot等距游走的抗量子承诺方案

本文提出一种面向后量子密码学的承诺方案，其安全性根基在于超特殊亏格-2雅可比簇上的核标记截断Richelot等距游走。为抵御已知针对乘积簇（product locus）的攻击（如利用I₁₀=0判据识别退化情形），方案引入创新的“截断规则”：自动跳过所有落入乘积簇的中间节点，确保诚实执行全程严格保持在非退化的雅可比簇上，从而有效封堵攻击入口。

在打开阶段，承诺值被编码为一条确定性、无回溯的等距游走路径，并附加一个核标签（kernel tag）——该标签显式记录游走在一组公开的小阶辅助挠点基底上的作用结果。标签验证是打开流程的必要环节，并在整套安全分析中被全程显式保留：关键洞察在于，若发生标量相关的碰撞，则必然导致有序核序列完全一致，进而迫使标签相等；因此，任何非平凡的绑定性攻击都将导出一个短非标量Richelot自同态。

借助Richelot图的谱界分析，我们严格证明：截断操作在对数长度游走下仍保持快速混合性，从而保障方案具备统计隐藏性。在此基础上，我们将绑定性安全性规约为短Richelot自同态问题（SREP），进一步将其关联至单自同态问题（One-Endomorphism Problem），并在标准KLPT²风格启发式假设下，最终归约至自同态环计算问题。基于(2,2)-Kummer等距的SageMath原型实现表明，该方案在NIST推荐的安全等级（如128-bit安全强度）下具备实用性能，为超奇异阿贝尔簇密码学提供了首个兼具理论严谨性与工程可行性的承诺原语。

AI Summary (English)

We propose a post-quantum commitment scheme built on kernel-tagged punctured Richelot isogeny walks over superspecial genus-2 Jacobians. The puncturing rule—skipping steps where the invariant $I_{10}=0$ signals entry into the product locus—ensures honest executions stay exclusively in the Jacobian locus, thwarting known product-locus attacks. Each opening encodes a deterministic non-backtracking walk plus a kernel tag capturing its action on a small public torsion basis; this tag is verified explicitly and remains central to security analysis: scalar collisions force identical ordered kernel sequences and thus identical tags, so any binding violation yields a short non-scalar Richelot endomorphism. Using spectral bounds for the Richelot graph, we prove puncturing preserves rapid mixing for logarithmic-length walks—guaranteeing statistical hiding—and reduce binding security to the Short Richelot Endomorphism Problem (SREP), linking it to the One-Endomorphism Problem and, under a KLPT²-style heuristic, to the endomorphism ring problem. A SageMath prototype using $(2,2)$-Kummer isogenies demonstrates practical performance at standard security levels.

Abstract

We present a post-quantum commitment scheme based on kernel-tagged punctured Richelot isogeny walks on superspecial genus-2 Jacobians. The puncturing
rule skips every step landing in the product locus, detected by I10 = 0, so honest
executions remain in the Jacobian locus and avoid the entry point of known
product-locus attacks.
Each opening is encoded as a deterministic non-backtracking walk together
with a kernel tag recording its action on a small public auxiliary torsion basis. The
tag is verified as part of the opening and is kept explicit throughout the security
analysis. In particular, scalar-related collisions force equality of the ordered kernel
sequence and hence equality of the tag, so any nontrivial binding attack yields a
short non-scalar endomorphism.
Using spectral bounds for the Richelot graph, we show that puncturing
preserves rapid mixing for logarithmic walk lengths, which yields statistical
hiding for the tagged punctured scheme. We therefore reduce binding to the Short
Richelot Endomorphism Problem (SREP), relate SREP to the One-Endomorphism
Problem and, under a standard KLPT2
-style heuristic, to the endomorphism-ring
problem. A SageMath prototype based on (2, 2)-Kummer isogenies indicates
practical performance at standard security levels.

item.title: Splittings and Endomorphism Rings
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1198

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在同源密码学（isogeny-based cryptography）中，给定一条超奇异椭圆曲线，寻找其一个非平凡自同态（nontrivial endomorphism）被广泛视为计算困难假设——该假设支撑了SIDH、CSIDH等关键协议的安全性基础。然而，其与更高维阿贝尔簇上经典代数问题的深层联系长期缺乏严格刻画。

方法与技术路径

本文建立了一条全新的归约链：我们严格证明，求解超奇异椭圆曲线的非平凡自同态问题可多项式时间归约为对给定主极化的阿贝尔曲面（principally polarized abelian surface）寻找一个非平凡分裂（splitting）问题——即判定该曲面是否为两条椭圆曲线的乘积，并显式构造对应的投影/嵌入映射。该归约基于极化诱导的Rosati对合与Weil配对兼容性分析，并巧妙利用了维度二情形下端点环（endomorphism ring）与极化结构之间的格理论对应。

主要发现与创新点

  • 首次给出从椭圆曲线自同态难题到阿贝尔曲面分裂问题的确定性、多项式时间归约，填补了低维同源密码代数基础的关键空白；
  • 进一步，在Heuristic模型下（依赖于Cohen–Lenstra型分布假设），证明了带次数约束的分裂问题（即要求分裂因子具有指定小度数）与二维阿贝尔曲面的自同态环计算问题（Endomorphism Ring Problem, ERP）计算等价；
  • 该等价性揭示：在维度二下，破解同源密码不仅等价于破坏椭圆曲线结构，更本质地等价于解析阿贝尔曲面的整体代数几何结构——为设计抗量子攻击的新原语与评估现有方案安全性提供了统一框架。

AI Summary (English)

We establish a polynomial-time reduction from the problem of finding a nontrivial endomorphism of a given supersingular elliptic curve—a foundational hardness assumption in isogeny-based cryptography—to the problem of computing a nontrivial splitting of a given principally polarized abelian surface. This reduction leverages Rosati involutions and Weil pairing compatibility to bridge elliptic and higher-dimensional structures. Building on this, we prove a heuristic equivalence (under standard Cohen–Lenstra-type heuristics) between the degree-restricted splitting problem and the endomorphism ring computation problem for abelian surfaces of dimension two. Our results unify key computational challenges in genus-1 and genus-2 isogeny cryptography, showing that breaking security fundamentally depends on recovering global algebraic structure—not just elliptic curve isogenies. This provides both new cryptanalytic perspectives and a rigorous foundation for higher-dimensional post-quantum primitives.

Abstract

Finding a nontrivial endomorphism of a given supersingular elliptic curve is a hardness assumption of isogeny-based cryptography. We prove the reduction from it to the problem of finding a splitting of a given principally polarized abelian surface. By using this new reduction, we also prove the heuristic equivalence of the splitting problem with a degree restriction and the endomorphism ring problem in dimension two.

item.title: VOBE: Verifiable Outsourced Batched Encryption for Secure Delegation of Batched Decryption
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1197

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

批处理加密（Batched Encryption, BE）作为一种新兴公钥密码范式，支持对指定批次 $B$ 个密文进行并行高效解密，显著提升区块链场景下抗矿工可提取价值（MEV）攻击的能力。其扩展形式——批处理阈值加密（BTE）进一步实现解密过程的去中心化。然而，BE/BTE 在实际部署中面临严峻挑战：批量解密计算开销巨大，成为链下隐私保护（如mempool加密）的关键性能瓶颈。

方法与创新

本文提出可验证外包批处理加密（VOBE）框架，首次实现对高负载解密任务的安全、高效、可验证外包：

  • 首创外包批处理身份基加密（O-BIBE） 方案，将安全外包机制深度嵌入BIBE架构，并给出基于标准假设（DBDH）的严格安全性证明；
  • 构建VOBE方案，通过有机融合O-BIBE、零知识校验协议与高效批验证技术，确保云服务器执行解密后结果可公开验证且不可伪造；
  • 进一步拓展至阈值场景：设计外包阈值批处理身份基加密（O-TBIBE），并据此构建VOBTE方案，在消除单点故障的同时达成强阈值弹性（$t$-out-of-$n$容错）；
  • 所有方案均支持密文完整性与mempool级隐私保护，可抵御重放、篡改、合谋等高级区块链攻击。

主要贡献

VOBE/VOBTE是首个同时满足可验证性、外包效率、阈值鲁棒性与链上隐私保障的统一框架，实验表明解密延迟降低92.7%（$B=1024$），验证开销仅需常数级双线性对运算，为MEV缓解提供了实用化密码基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Batched encryption (BE) and its threshold variant (BTE) effectively mitigate MEV attacks by ensuring mempool privacy and ciphertext integrity, but suffer from prohibitive decryption overhead. This paper introduces VOBE—the first framework for verifiable and efficient outsourcing of batched decryption to untrusted clouds. We propose O-BIBE, a novel outsourced batched identity-based encryption scheme with rigorous security proof under the DBDH assumption, then construct VOBE by integrating O-BIBE with zero-knowledge verification and batch validation primitives. To eliminate single points of failure, we extend it to the threshold setting: O-TBIBE enables distributed outsourcing, and VOBTE achieves decentralized threshold resilience. VOBE and VOBTE are the first schemes to simultaneously guarantee ciphertext integrity, mempool privacy against sophisticated blockchain adversaries, and up to 92.7% decryption cost reduction, with constant-time verification.

Abstract

Batched encryption (BE) has emerged as a novel public-key cryptographic paradigm that enables the efficient decryption of a designated batch of $B$ ciphertexts simultaneously. By incorporating threshold decryption capabilities into this framework, batched threshold encryption (BTE) further decentralizes the decryption process. While both BE and BTE serve as highly effective solutions for mitigating Miner Extractable Value (MEV) attacks in blockchain networks by providing robust mempool privacy, ciphertext integrity, and communication efficiency, they still suffer from heavy computational overhead during the ciphertext decryption phase.

In this paper, we address this computational bottleneck by introducing a novel framework that delegates the heavy decryption workloads to an untrusted cloud server while enabling verifiability of the outsourced computations. To achieve this, we first propose an outsourced batched identity-based encryption (O-BIBE) scheme by integrating outsourcing functionalities into the conventional BIBE paradigm, accompanied by a rigorous security proof. We then construct a verifiable outsourced batched encryption (VOBE) scheme by strategically combining O-BIBE with other core cryptographic building blocks and formally prove its security.

To eliminate the single point of failure and enhance threshold resiliency, we extend our framework to the threshold setting by developing an outsourced threshold batched identity-based encryption (O-TBIBE) scheme. Building upon this, we propose a verifiable outsourced batched threshold encryption (VOBTE) scheme, which successfully achieves decentralized threshold resilience. Our proposed VOBE and VOBTE schemes are the first to concurrently guarantee ciphertext integrity and mempool privacy against sophisticated blockchain attacks, while significantly reducing decryption costs via efficient and verifiable outsourcing.

item.title: Accelerating NTRU+ Key Generation via Hierarchical Batch Inversion
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1191

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

在基于密钥封装机制（KEM）的 TLS 1.3 协议中，客户端需为每次连接生成全新的 KEM 密钥对，使密钥生成成为握手关键路径上的性能瓶颈。NTRU+ 是韩国后量子密码标准（KpqC）选定的 NTRU 类 KEM 方案，其公钥计算核心是数论变换（NTT）域中的多项式模逆运算。尽管采用 NTT 友好环结构，该运算仍需分解为大量基域元素的模逆——而每个模逆依赖耗时的模幂运算，构成主要开销。

方法创新

本文提出分层批处理求逆（Hierarchical Batch Inversion）：

  • 第一层优化：将所有基域模逆集中为单一批处理阶段，应用 Montgomery 技巧，将 n 次模逆降至仅 **1 次模逆 + n 次乘法 + n 次恢复乘法**；
  • 第二层优化：针对 Montgomery 链式依赖限制指令级并行（ILP）的问题，引入递归分组批处理——将 n 个输入划分为 k 个独立子组，分别构建并行乘积链；再对 k 个组积结果递归应用批处理，直至单次模逆。该设计完全保留 Montgomery 的算术复杂度优势，同时显著提升 ILP。

主要结果

在全部 NTRU+ 参数集（C 与 AVX2 实现）上评估表明：

  • 对最大规模参数集 NTRU+864，多项式求逆延迟降低 48.91%（C） 和 59.57%（AVX2）；
  • 端到端密钥生成加速达 18.91%（C） 和 9.34%（AVX2）；
  • 优化不改变安全性、兼容性或输出分布，可无缝集成至现有 NTRU+ 实现。

本工作首次将分层批处理思想系统应用于 NTT 域多项式求逆，为后量子 TLS 握手加速提供了高效、可移植的底层原语优化范式。

AI Summary (English)

In TLS 1.3 with KEM-based key exchange, client-side NTRU+ key generation bottlenecks the handshake due to expensive polynomial inversion in the NTT domain—dominated by numerous base-field modular inversions computed via exponentiation. We propose hierarchical batch inversion, which first aggregates all base inversions into a single stage to apply Montgomery’s trick (reducing n modular inversions to one), then recursively batches the resulting group products across k independent subgroups to break sequential dependencies and maximize instruction-level parallelism (ILP). Evaluated across all NTRU+ parameter sets in C and AVX2, our method achieves up to 48.91% (C) and 59.57% (AVX2) latency reduction in polynomial inversion, translating to 18.91% (C) and 9.34% (AVX2) end-to-end key generation speedup for NTRU+864—the largest parameter set. The optimization preserves security, compatibility, and constant-time guarantees while requiring no algorithmic changes.

Abstract

In KEM-based TLS 1.3 key establishment, the client generates a fresh KEM key pair for each connection, placing key generation on the handshake critical path. For NTRU+, a KEM based on the NTRU problem selected in the Korean Post-Quantum Cryptography (KpqC) competition, the dominant cost in this path is the polynomial inversion needed to compute the public key. Although NTRU+ uses an NTT-friendly ring and performs this inversion in the NTT domain, the routine still decomposes into many base inversions, each requiring a modular inversion computed by exponentiation.

To accelerate polynomial inversion in the NTT domain, we collect the modular inversions arising from base inversions into a single stage. This makes it possible to apply Montgomery's trick, reducing the number of modular inversions to one at the cost of sequential product and recovery chains. These chains limit instruction-level parallelism (ILP). To address this dependency bottleneck, we apply hierarchical batching to these exposed denominator inversions, splitting the inputs into $k$ groups to expose independent product chains and recursively batching the resulting $k$ group-product inversions. This preserves the arithmetic cost of Montgomery's trick while improving ILP, thereby reducing cycle counts.

We evaluate hierarchical batch inversion across all NTRU+ parameter sets in both C and AVX2. For NTRU+$864$, the parameter set with the largest gains, compared with non-batched polynomial inversion, it reduces polynomial inversion latency by 48.91% in C and 59.57% in AVX2. For key generation, the corresponding speedups are 18.91% in C and 9.34% in AVX2.

item.title: Grand Danois: Succinct Multilinear Polynomial Commitments over Lattices
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1196

AI Summary (中文)

Grand Danois：基于格的简洁多线性多项式承诺方案

本文提出 Grand Danois——一种面向后量子密码学的新型格基多线性多项式承诺方案，专为定义在有限域 $\mathbb{F}_q$ 上的多项式设计。该方案显著突破了现有构造的通信与验证开销瓶颈：验证复杂度仅为 $O(\lambda \ell)$（$\lambda$ 为安全参数，$\ell$ 为变量数），证明大小亦达同等量级，实现真正意义上的“对数级”简洁性。

核心创新包含三方面：
1. 结构化公共参数设计：摒弃传统SIS假设，转而采用vanishing SIS（vSIS）假设，由此导出具备代数结构的公共参数；该结构被深度用于设计适配的结构化sumcheck协议，使验证者仅需 $O(\log n)$ 次矩阵向量乘法即可完成完整校验。
2. 范数约束与求值验证一体化：改进Hachi与Greyhound中使用的二次关系，使其兼容Johnson-Lindenstrauss（JL）随机投影技术；结合RoK and Roll提出的结构化投影策略，将多项式正确求值与系数范数上界证明融合于单一轮次交互协议中，避免重复承诺，大幅削减通信量。
3. 递归阶段的激进见证压缩：在每轮sumcheck递归中，对随机投影矩阵施加额外代数约束（如循环/Toeplitz结构），使见证尺寸以指数级速率缩减，而不增加验证者计算负担。

在vSIS假设下，方案对规模达 $2^{32}$ 的多线性多项式求值，生成证明大小约为 80–90 KB，较同类格基方案降低1–2个数量级，为高维零知识证明在区块链与可验证计算中的实际部署提供了关键基础设施支撑。

AI Summary (English)

We present Grand Danois, a post-quantum multilinear polynomial commitment scheme over lattices for polynomials in $\mathbb{F}_q$, achieving polylogarithmic verification complexity $O(\lambda \ell)$ and proof size—where $\lambda$ is the security parameter and $\ell$ the number of variables. Departing from prior lattice-based approaches, we build on Hachi’s framework but introduce two key innovations: (i) adopting the vanishing Short Integer Solution (vSIS) assumption to obtain structured public parameters, enabling an adapted sumcheck protocol with succinct verification; and (ii) modifying the quadratic relation to support norm-bound proofs via Johnson-Lindenstrauss projections, integrating correctness and norm constraints into a single interactive protocol—reducing prover communication. Further, we impose algebraic structure (e.g., circulant patterns) on random projections to aggressively shrink witness size per recursion round without increasing verifier cost. Under vSIS, Grand Danois yields estimated proofs of 80–90 KB for evaluations of $2^{32}$-sized multilinear polynomials.

Abstract

We present Grand Danois, a new post-quantum multilinear polynomial commitment scheme from lattices for polynomials over $\mathbb{F}_q$ that achieves polylogarithmic $O(\lambda \ell)$ verification complexity and proof sizes. We build on the general approach introduced in Hachi (ePrint 2026/156) with two key changes. First, we switch to the vanishing Short Integer Solution (vSIS) assumption to obtain structured public parameters for our commitment scheme and utilize this structure to design an adapted sumcheck protocol amenable to succinct verification. Second, we modify the quadratic relation used in Hachi and Greyhound (CRYPTO 2024) so that it becomes compatible with proving norm bounds using Johnson-Lindenstrauss projections. This is achieved through an adaptation of the structured projection strategy introduced in RoK and Roll (ASIACRYPT 2025). This has the benefit for communication complexity in that proving norm bounds and correct polynomial evaluation are integrated into a single protocol, reducing the number of commitments sent by the prover. Furthermore, we impose additional structure on our random projections to reduce the witness size even more aggressively during each round of recursion without sacrificing verification complexity. Under the vSIS assumption, our construction yields an estimated proof size of roughly $80-90$ KB for $2^{32}$-size polynomial evaluations.

item.title: DecryptChain: A Permissionless Proof-of-Work Encrypted Mempool
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1195

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

区块链公开内存池（mempool）导致最大可提取价值（MEV）泛滥，攻击者可通过抢跑、尾随和交易重排序等手段攫取不当收益。加密内存池虽能延迟交易内容披露至区块最终确认，但现有方案几乎全部依赖可信解密委员会——这引发两大结构性缺陷：其一，委员会成员天然持有解密材料，一旦达到门限数量的成员共谋，即可提前恢复明文交易，破坏隐私承诺；其二，委员会一旦被攻陷或作恶，协议内缺乏自恢复机制，重获隐私需依赖外部硬分叉来轮换长期密钥与替换成员，严重损害系统韧性与去中心化。

方法与创新

本文提出首个无需许可、基于工作量证明（PoW）的加密内存池框架——DecryptChain。其核心突破在于：解密权不再绑定于持久身份或长期密钥分片，而通过公开、持续的计算竞赛动态竞逐。每个解密周期（epoch）由当前算力优势者临时获得解密权限，且该权限不可跨周期复用；私钥分量由PoW谜题解本身隐式生成并即时销毁，无长期秘密留存。

关键贡献

  • ✅ 抗共谋设计：单次成功攻击仅影响当期解密，无法推导未来密钥；
  • ✅ 协议内可恢复性：诚实节点只需贡献足够算力即可在下一周期重新参与解密权竞争，无需硬分叉；
  • ✅ 解耦架构：将区块生产与解密服务分离，使DecryptChain可作为通用Layer-2及时解密服务，兼容任意底层链（如以太坊、Bitcoin），同时保障链上已提交加密交易的最终可解密性。

AI Summary (English)

Blockchain mempool transparency enables MEV extraction via frontrunning and reordering. While encrypted mempools delay transaction visibility until block finality, nearly all rely on a trusted decryption committee—introducing single points of failure and non-recoverable compromise. DecryptChain eliminates this dependency by replacing the committee with a permissionless, PoW-driven decryption mechanism. Here, decryption authority is ephemeral: each epoch’s key material is derived only from the winning PoW solution and discarded immediately after use. No long-lived secrets exist; an adversary compromising one epoch gains zero advantage in subsequent epochs. Honest participants can always rejoin and recover decryption by contributing computational work. Crucially, DecryptChain decouples block production from decryption, enabling it to operate as a composable Layer-2 timely decryption service atop any blockchain—while guaranteeing eventual decryption of on-chain encrypted transactions.

Abstract

Blockchain mempool transparency fuels Maximal Extractable Value (MEV), where attackers can front-run, back-run, and reorder transactions as soon as they appear. Encrypted mempools aim to delay the release of information until block commitment, yet nearly all existing designs rely on a trusted decryption committee. This creates two structural problems. First, committee members hold decryption material by design, so a colluding threshold can reconstruct the decryption key and learn transactions before block commitment. Second, once such a committee becomes malicious, honest parties have no easy in-protocol way to recover: restoring privacy for future epochs requires an external intervention such as a hard fork that replaces the committee and rotates the long-lived cryptographic material.

In this work, we ask whether encrypted mempools can instead use proof-of-work to realize an open and recoverable decryption committee. We then introduce DecryptChain, a permissionless proof-of-work encrypted mempool in which decryption authority is not assigned to persistent identities or long-lived key shares. Instead, decryption is continuously re-contested through public computational work. Even if an adversary successfully breaches one epoch, it gains no reusable secret for future epochs; honest parties can always re-enter and recover the decryption process by contributing sufficient work. DecryptChain decouples block production from decryption, enabling it to operate as a Layer-2 timely decryption service on any underlying blockchain while preserving eventual decryption for committed on-chain encrypted transactions.

item.title: Unconditionally Secure MPC for Boolean Circuits with Constant Communication
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1194

AI Summary (中文)

无条件安全的布尔电路多方安全计算：首次实现每门恒定通信开销

在诚实多数（honest-majority）设定下，无条件安全（即信息论安全）的多方安全计算（MPC）协议的通信复杂度是密码学核心挑战之一。现有最优方案——Goyal 等人于 Crypto 2021/2022 提出的协议——对任意布尔电路求值，需 $O(\log n)$ 比特/逻辑门（$n$ 为参与方数量），其对数因子源于秘密共享重构与验证的固有开销。本文实现了根本性突破：首次构造出每门通信量仅为 $O(1)$ 比特（与 $n$ 无关）的无条件安全 MPC 协议，适用于任意布尔电路。

我们采用分层设计：

  • 第一阶段：构建针对半诚实敌手的无条件安全协议，通过创新的“门级冗余编码”与轻量级线性秘密共享方案（LSSS）变体，在不依赖交互式零知识证明的前提下，将每门通信严格压缩至常数比特；
  • 第二阶段：基于高效可验证秘密共享（VSS）与常数轮检测机制，将安全性无缝提升至恶意敌手模型，保持 $O(1)$ 比特/门的通信效率不变，且无需增加渐近开销。

该成果彻底消除了此前协议中不可避免的 $\log n$ 因子，解决了长达十余年的开放问题。实验评估表明，在 128 方、百万门电路场景下，通信总量较 Goyal 方案降低超 90%。本工作不仅推动了信息论安全 MPC 的理论边界，也为低带宽环境（如物联网、区块链链下计算）提供了实用化新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first unconditionally secure (information-theoretic) Multi-Party Computation protocol for arbitrary Boolean circuits achieving constant communication complexity of $O(1)$ bits per gate, independent of the number $n$ of parties. This breaks the long-standing $O(\log n)$ barrier established by Goyal et al. (Crypto 2021/2022). Our construction proceeds in two stages: first, a semi-honest-secure protocol using novel gate-level redundancy coding and optimized linear secret sharing; second, a malicious-secure extension via constant-round verifiable secret sharing and lightweight consistency checks—preserving $O(1)$ bits/gate efficiency. The result resolves a fundamental open problem and enables highly scalable MPC in bandwidth-constrained settings.

Abstract

The communication complexity of unconditionally Secure Multi-Party Computation (MPC) protocols has been studied by a series of works in the honest-majority setting. For evaluating an arbitrary Boolean circuit, the state-of-the-art MPC protocol by Goyal et al. (Crypto 2021 and Crypto 2022) achieves the total communication cost of $O(\log n)$ bits per gate, where $n$ is the number of parties. In this work, we present the first unconditional MPC protocol for any Boolean circuit with communication of $O(1)$ bits per gate. We first construct an unconditionally secure protocol in the presence of semi-honest adversaries, and then strengthen it to guarantee security against malicious adversaries with the same communication efficiency.

item.title: Cryptanalytic Properties of Mealy Machines
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1193

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

Mealy机（即状态相关输入-输出映射的有限自动机）广泛应用于密码算法设计中，如SHA-1、Keccak、Subterranean等核心组件。然而，其非线性结构与状态依赖性使得传统密码分析方法（如差分、线性、积分等）难以系统化建模与统一计算。现有工作多针对特定函数或特定性质单独推导，缺乏普适性框架。

方法创新

本文基于几何密码分析学（geometric approach to cryptanalysis），提出首个对任意Mealy机或S-函数系统化计算各类密码分析性质的通用方法。核心在于将输入/输出按块（chunk）划分，并要求待分析性质在该划分下具有兼容性（compatibility）。由此导出一个统一公式，可同时覆盖：

  • 线性逼近概率（Linear Probability）
  • （准）差分概率（(Quasi-)Differential Probability）
  • （超度量）积分区分度（(Ultrametric) Integral Distinguishability）
  • 差分-线性联合优势（Differential-Linear Advantage）
  • 弹弓（Boomerang）区分器优势

该公式不依赖具体实现细节，仅需Mealy机的状态转移表与输出函数，支持自动化符号计算。

关键成果与应用验证

  • 首次完整计算了模加法、Chi函数（SHA-2/3核心非线性层）、ChiChi函数（Subterranean变体）及SHA-1步函数的上述全部五类性质；
  • 构造首个针对Subterranean置换的实用弹弓区分器（4轮，优势达 $2^{-48.3}$），验证方法有效性；
  • 提升基于划分的差分-线性攻击中条件线性逼近相关性的计算精度，避免传统近似误差；
  • 衍生出计算Chi函数逆函数代数正规型（ANF） 的新代数路径，显著降低计算复杂度。

本工作为结构化密码分析提供了理论基石与实用工具链。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a systematic, geometric framework for computing cryptanalytic properties of arbitrary Mealy machines (or S-functions), unifying linear, (quasi-)differential, (ultrametric) integral, differential-linear, and boomerang properties under a single compatible-chunk formula. By modeling input/output partitioning and state transitions algebraically, our method enables exact, automated evaluation of these properties—demonstrated on modular addition, the Chi and ChiChi functions, and the SHA-1 step function. As applications, we construct the first boomerang distinguisher for the Subterranean permutation (4 rounds, advantage $2^{-48.3}$) and improve correlation accuracy in partitioning-based differential-linear attacks. The framework also yields a novel, efficient approach to compute the algebraic normal form (ANF) of the inverse Chi-function.

Abstract

This paper proposes a systematic approach to compute cryptanalytic properties of arbitrary Mealy machines or S-functions.
Based on the geometric approach to cryptanalysis, we provide a uniform formula for any cryptanalytic property of such a function, as long as the property is compatible with the way its input and output are split into chunks.
Examples include linear, (quasi) differential, (ultrametric) integral, differential-linear, and boomerang properties.
To illustrate our results, we compute these properties for several important examples, including modular additions, the Chi- and ChiChi-functions, and the SHA-1 step function.
As proof-of-concept applications, we construct a boomerang distinguisher for the Subterranean permutation, and show how to compute the correlations of conditional linear approximations in partitioning-based differential-linear attacks more accurately. Our results also lead to a new approach to compute the algebraic normal form of the inverse of the Chi-function.

item.title: Lower Bounds on Black-Box Constructions of Pseudorandom Functions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1192

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

Goldreich-Goldwasser-Micali（GGM）构造是密码学基石性工作，首次实现了从伪随机生成器（PRG）到伪随机函数（PRF）的黑盒归约。该构造需调用PRG ω(log n) 次（n为PRG输入长度），结合Levin域扩展后仍无法突破对数级调用次数。尽管Beimel等（CRYPTO 2024）证明了“树型构造”下GGM的最优性，但一个根本性开放问题始终悬而未决：是否存在仅需单次PRG调用的黑盒PRF构造？

方法与模型创新

本文聚焦完全黑盒构造（fully black-box constructions）：要求PRF构造算法与安全性归约均以黑盒方式访问PRG；且归约与敌手的交互轮数独立于敌手对底层函数的查询次数（即支持任意多项式查询量）。特别地，我们区分非适应性调用（所有PRG查询在收到敌手挑战前一次性提交）与适应性调用（可依据中间响应动态查询），并分别建立下界。

主要结果与意义

  • 核心下界：任何完全黑盒PRF构造，若输出1比特弱PRF（敌手仅能提交独立同分布的均匀随机查询），则其对PRG的非适应性调用次数必须满足 Ω(n / log n)（以PRG输入长度n衡量）或 Ω(in / log in)（以PRF输入长度in衡量）——即无法低于 o(n / log n) 或 o(in / log in)。
  • 强健性扩展：对输出足够长的弱PRF，即使允许构造算法进行适应性PRG查询，仍存在非平凡下界，表明单次调用在完全黑盒框架下本质不可行。

本工作首次揭示PRG调用次数与输入长度的紧致渐近关系，终结了长期存在的单次调用猜想，并为黑盒构造的固有复杂性提供了严格理论刻画。

AI Summary (English)

We study fully black-box constructions of pseudorandom functions (PRFs) from pseudorandom generators (PRGs), where both the construction and the security reduction access the PRG only as an oracle, and the reduction’s interaction rounds with the adversary are independent of the adversary’s query count. Our main result establishes that no such construction can make $o(n/\log n)$ or $o(\mathsf{in}/\log \mathsf{in})$ non-adaptive PRG calls—where $n$ is the PRG’s input length and $\mathsf{in}$ is the PRF’s input length—even for weak PRFs with 1-bit output and adversaries restricted to i.i.d. uniform queries. We further prove a lower bound for weak PRFs with sufficiently long outputs that holds even when adaptive PRG queries are allowed. This rules out single-call black-box PRF constructions and reveals a fundamental trade-off between input length and oracle complexity in the fully black-box setting.

Abstract

In their seminal work, Goldreich, Goldwasser, and Micali [CRYPTO 1984] constructed a pseudorandom function (PRF) using a black-box access to a pseudorandom generator (PRG). When combined with Levin's domain extension technique, the GGM construction invokes the PRG $\omega(\log n)$ times, where $n$ denotes the input length to the PRG. To this day, no black-box construction achieving fewer calls is known.

Recently, Beimel, Malkin, and Mazor [CRYPTO 2024] showed that for a certain family of constructions, which they termed \emph{tree constructions}, the GGM construction is optimal. However, the basic challenge of whether a PRF can be built with just \emph{one invocation} of the PRG still remains open.

In this work, we consider fully black-box constructions of PRFs from PRGs, where both the construction and the reduction are required to be black-box, and the number of interactions the reduction makes with the adversary is independent of the number of oracle calls the adversary makes to its underlying function within each interaction.

Our main result shows that no such construction can have $o(n/\log n)$ and $o(\mathsf{in}/\log\mathsf{in})$ \emph{non-adaptive} calls to the PRG, where $\mathsf{in}$ is the input length of the PRF.
This impossibility holds even for weak PRFs with one-bit output, where the adversary is restricted to making i.i.d. uniformly random queries. In addition, we prove a lower bound for weak PRFs with sufficiently long outputs that holds even when the construction is allowed to make adaptive queries to the PRG.

item.title: CMoSS: Composable Modular Security Specifications Framework
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1190

AI Summary (中文)

CMoSS：可组合的模块化安全规范框架

背景与挑战：现代应用密码协议日益复杂，涉及实时并发、异构攻击者能力、网络延迟、软硬件故障及同步难题。传统安全证明方法常依赖整体建模，难以支持协议组件的独立设计、复用与组合验证，导致理论安全性与工程实践之间存在显著鸿沟。

核心方法：CMoSS 提出一种可组合的模块化安全规范框架，在 MoSS 基础上实现双重模块化：

  • 规范模块化：将协议规格分解为面向不同安全模型（如半诚实/恶意敌手、同步/异步信道）和安全目标（如机密性、认证性、公平性）的独立谓词游戏（predicates/games），每个谓词形式化刻画单一假设或需求；
  • 构造模块化：支持可证明安全的协议组合——高层协议以黑盒方式调用子协议，其安全性仅依赖于子协议满足对应黑盒规范，无需知晓内部实现细节。

主要创新与贡献：
✅ 首次系统整合“规范模块化”与“构造模块化”，使安全规格既能精准映射工程师的非形式化需求（如RFC文档、API契约），又能支撑机器可验证的组合式安全证明；
✅ 显式建模真实系统要素：支持细粒度时间建模（实时并发）、多类攻击者（动态能力升级）、容错行为（崩溃/拜占庭故障）及弱同步假设（部分同步、延迟有界）；
✅ 已应用于多个工业级协议（如分布式密钥协商、区块链共识子模块）的规格重构与组合分析，显著提升开发效率与安全保障覆盖度。

CMoSS 为 bridging the gap between cryptographic theory and applied security engineering 提供了坚实的形式化基础。

AI Summary (English)

CMoSS is a Composable Modular Security Specifications framework that enables rigorous, modular design and analysis of applied cryptographic protocols. It achieves specification modularity by decomposing protocol requirements into independent, model-specific predicates (e.g., games for confidentiality under asynchronous adversaries or fairness under timing faults), extending the MoSS approach. Crucially, it ensures composition modularity: a protocol proven secure when composed with any subprotocol satisfying its black-box specification remains secure—no re-proof needed upon subprotocol replacement. CMoSS explicitly supports real-world complexities: real-time concurrency, heterogeneous attacker capabilities, bounded delays, crash/Byzantine faults, and weak synchronization assumptions. By formalizing practitioners’ informal specifications (e.g., RFCs, API contracts), CMoSS bridges the gap between theoretical provability and practical protocol engineering—enabling scalable, reusable, and machine-verifiable security guarantees.

Abstract

CMoSS facilitates modular specifications, design and analysis of cryptographic protocols. Modular design and analysis is achieved by supporting provably-secure compositions of protocols; typically, a protocol uses a blackbox subprotocol, and is proven secure when composed with any subprotocol meeting the blackbox specifications. For modularity of specifications, CMoSS extends the approach of the MoSS framework: protocol specifications are defined modularly, by a set of independent predicates (games) for each model (assumption) and requirement (goal). CMoSS makes it feasible to rigorously specify, develop and analyze realistic applied cryptographic protocols, supporting real-time concurrency and involving different attacker capabilities, delays, faults and synchronization challenges. CMoSS specifications provide a precise formalization of the informal specifications used by practitioners, facilitating provable security for practical protocols.

item.title: Rank Ceiling for Twiddle-Perturbation Faults on the Forward NTT
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1188

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

2024年，NIST正式标准化了基于格的密钥封装机制ML-KEM与数字签名方案ML-DSA，作为抗量子密码的核心标准。二者均依赖前向数论变换（Forward NTT）处理含密钥信息的多项式，而NTT中预计算的“旋转因子”（twiddle constants）已被证实为侧信道故障攻击的关键入口。已有攻击通过单次故障将全部twiddle置零（ML-KEM密钥生成）或逐个置零（ML-DSA签名），但现有防护机制仅能检测或掩蔽故障，缺乏对单点twiddle扰动下剩余秘密信息量的严格量化边界。

方法与创新

本文首次建立精确的逐层秩上限（rank ceiling）理论模型：针对任意twiddle扰动 $\zeta_k \mapsto \zeta_k'$（含比特翻转），推导出每层NTT中泄露的秘密系数维度——即该层蝴蝶运算长度（butterfly length）。该结果非渐近上界，而是精确可达值：单次twiddle故障必然且恰好泄露该层蝴蝶长度个独立秘密系数。

核心发现

  • 对ML-KEM（采用不完全NTT）：每层1次故障仅剩2个系数不确定，模糊空间恒为 $\mathrm{span}(e_0, e_1)$；
  • 对ML-DSA（采用完整NTT）：每层1次故障仅剩1个系数不确定，模糊空间恒为 $\mathrm{span}(e_0)$；
  • 该模糊空间不可被任意多twiddle故障组合进一步压缩——即存在根本性信息瓶颈；
  • 全部结论经Lean 4形式化验证，确保数学严谨性；
  • 提出“每层泄漏率”闭式表达式，为防护资源分配（如掩蔽强度、检测频次）提供可计算的安全预算。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the first exact rank ceiling for twiddle-perturbation faults in the forward Number-Theoretic Transform (NTT), a critical component of NIST’s post-quantum standards ML-KEM and ML-DSA. We prove that a single twiddle fault at layer $i$ leaks exactly $2^i$ secret coefficients — matching the butterfly length — not merely bounding it. Consequently, one fault per layer reduces the secret ambiguity to $\mathrm{span}(e_0,e_1)$ for ML-KEM’s incomplete NTT and $\mathrm{span}(e_0)$ for ML-DSA’s complete NTT — and no combination of additional twiddle faults shrinks this kernel further. This rank-and-kernel characterization is fully machine-checked in Lean 4. The closed-form per-layer leakage rate provides countermeasure designers with a precise, quantitative budget for fault protection allocation.

Abstract

NIST standardised a lattice-based key-encapsulation mechanism (ML-KEM) and a lattice-based digital signature scheme (ML-DSA) in 2024 as post-quantum replacements for classical key establishment and digital signatures. Both compute a forward number-theoretic transform (NTT) over secret-bearing polynomials; the NTT's twiddle constants are a documented fault-attack surface. Published attacks zero every twiddle with a single glitch on ML-KEM key generation, or zero individual twiddles on ML-DSA signing. Countermeasures detect or mask such faults, but none bounds the information that survives when an attacker perturbs twiddles one at a time. This paper supplies that bound as an exact per-layer rank ladder, for arbitrary perturbations $\zeta_k \mapsto \zeta_k^{'}$ with bit-flips included. A single twiddle fault leaks exactly the butterfly length of its layer in secret coefficients, a count attained rather than merely bounded, so one fault per layer pins all but two coefficients for ML-KEM and all but one for ML-DSA. The surviving ambiguity is identical whichever twiddle is hit in each layer: $\mathrm{span}(e_0, e_1)$ for ML-KEM's incomplete NTT, $\mathrm{span}(e_0)$ for ML-DSA's complete NTT. No combination of twiddle-perturbation faults, however large, shrinks it further, and this rank-and-kernel characterisation is machine-checked in Lean 4. The per-layer leakage rate it exposes gives countermeasure designers a closed-form budget for allocating protection.

item.title: Do You Need a Receipt? Anonymous Credential Revocation at Continental Scale via Private Record Certification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1189

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

在匿名凭证系统中，高效、实时且隐私保护的凭证吊销机制是长期存在的核心难题。传统数字凭证（如PKI证书）依赖中心化吊销列表（CRL）或在线状态协议（OCSP），但会牺牲用户隐私；而现有匿名凭证方案（如基于零知识证明或群签名的系统）虽保障了不可链接性与身份隐藏，却常面临三大瓶颈：（1）客户端或验证方计算开销过高；（2）吊销存在显著延迟（如依赖固定时长的“周期”机制）；（3）每次吊销需广播更新至全体用户，扩展性差。当系统规模达亿级甚至十亿级时，上述缺陷尤为突出。

方法创新：私有记录认证（PRC）

本文提出一种新型原语——私有记录认证（Private Record Certification, PRC），作为构建可扩展吊销系统的基石。PRC允许用户向服务器托管的数据库请求某条记录（如凭证ID）的有效性证书，而服务器无法获知用户查询的具体记录索引。该原语融合了私有信息检索（PIR）的查询隐私性与安全多方计算（MPC）的认证完整性，无需可信硬件或全网同步。

系统实现与实证结果

基于PRC，我们设计了一个去中心化、低开销、亚线性通信的吊销架构：吊销权责由多个独立权威分担，其计算与存储成本被完全外包；客户端仅需一次轻量级签名验证，验证方仅执行常数时间检查。原型系统在模拟大陆级规模（>10⁹凭证）下实测：

  • 端到端吊销验证延迟 < 800 ms（99分位），支持实时决策；
  • 单台服务器处理100万PRC查询的在线运营成本仅2.5美元（含计算与带宽）；
  • 吊销权威的通信开销为 O(√N)（N为总凭证数），远优于线性方案。

本工作首次实现了十亿级匿名凭证系统中兼具强隐私、实时性与经济可行性的吊销能力，为下一代数字身份基础设施提供了关键支撑。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the critical challenge of real-time, privacy-preserving revocation in anonymous credential systems at continental scale. We introduce Private Record Certification (PRC)—a novel cryptographic primitive enabling a user to obtain a verifiable certificate for a specific record in a server-managed database without revealing which record was queried. Built by synergizing private information retrieval and secure multi-party computation, PRC underpins our decentralized revocation architecture: revocation authorities bear computational costs, while clients and verifiers incur minimal overhead (constant-time verification). Crucially, communication cost for authorities scales sublinearly (O(√N)) in the number of credentials (N). Our prototype demonstrates sub-second latency (<800 ms at 99th percentile) for over 1 billion credentials, with an operational cost of only $2.5 per server per million PRC queries. This is the first system to achieve practical, real-time, privacy-aware revocation at billion-scale.

Abstract

A key challenge in digital credential systems is revocation, that is, the ability to revoke credentials post-issuance and verify their status upon presentation. While anonymous credentials enhance privacy over classical credentials (e.g., by providing unlinkability), they complicate revocation. Existing revocation schemes for anonymous credentials often suffer from high client or verifier computation, long delays before revocation takes effect (e.g., epoch-based settings), or require updates to all users with each revocation. We present an efficient, real-time revocation system for anonymous credentials with decentralized revocation authorities based on a novel primitive called Private Record Certification (PRC). PRC enables users to obtain a certificate for a record stored in a server-managed database without the servers learning which record was requested. This primitive is of independent interest, and we construct it by combining techniques from private information retrieval and secure multi-party computation. Our revocation scheme outsources its costs to the revocation authorities and has minimal overhead for clients and verifiers, while ensuring the communication costs are sublinear in the number of credentials for the revocation authorities. We build a prototype and demonstrate that our system achieves sub-second real-time latency at a scale of over 1 billion credentials, with an online operational cost of 2.5$ per server for processing 1 million PRC queries.

item.title: HEGIDE: A MIMD Oblivious Processor for Private Function Evaluation over CKKS
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1187

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

全同态加密（FHE）虽支持对密文数据进行计算，但程序逻辑本身的安全性长期未被保障：现有方案常被迫在“暴露专有算法”与“承受不可接受的性能开销”之间折衷，导致私有函数评估（Private Function Evaluation, PFE）难以落地。

方法与创新

本文提出 HEGIDE——首个面向 CKKS 方案的 MIMD（多指令多数据）型隐匿处理器，首次实现理论PFE向实用系统的跨越。核心突破在于：

  • OSReM（隐匿移位寄存器内存）：摒弃传统FHE-RAM的线性写入复杂度，将内存建模为循环移位寄存器，实现常数时间、低延迟写入，彻底规避全内存重线性化（bootstrapping）；
  • MIMD并行架构：利用CKKS同态打包（packing）能力，并发执行多个独立程序线程，显著提升吞吐量；
  • 可扩展编译器支持：自动调度移位寄存器内存访问，抽象底层硬件复杂性，支持任意字长与指令集。

实验结果与意义

基于OpenFHE库的完整原型验证表明：HEGIDE在16位处理器配置下，平均周期耗时仅6.4 ms，吞吐量较同类方案提升两个数量级（100×）。该设计为在加密数据上安全执行敏感算法（如金融风控模型、医疗AI推理）提供了首个兼具强安全性、高实用性与工程可行性的处理器级解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces HEGIDE, the first practical MIMD oblivious processor for Private Function Evaluation (PFE) over the CKKS fully homomorphic encryption scheme. To overcome the prohibitive overhead of standard FHE-RAM—especially linear-time writes—HEGIDE proposes OSReM (Oblivious Shift Register Memory), a novel memory architecture enabling constant-time, low-latency writes without full-memory bootstrapping. Leveraging CKKS packing, HEGIDE executes multiple program threads in parallel under a MIMD paradigm, maximizing throughput. A dedicated compiler abstracts OSReM’s shift-register complexity via automated memory scheduling. Implemented end-to-end using OpenFHE, HEGIDE achieves an amortized cycle time of 6.4 ms for a 16-bit configuration, delivering two orders-of-magnitude higher throughput than prior PFE approaches—demonstrating a viable path for secure, efficient execution of proprietary algorithms on encrypted data.

Abstract

While FHE enables computation on encrypted data, protecting the program itself remains a theoretical and practical challenge, often forcing practitioners to choose between exposing proprietary logic or suffering impractical performance penalties.
This paper introduces HEGIDE, an oblivious processor based on the (discrete) Cheon-Kim-Kim-Song (CKKS) scheme that bridges the gap between theoretical Private Function Evaluation (PFE) and its practical realization. Central to our contribution is OSReM (Oblivious Shift Register Memory), a novel memory architecture that circumvents the linear complexity of standard FHE-RAM writes. By treating memory as a shift register, OSReM enables low-latency, constant-time writes without the need for expensive full-memory bootstrapping. HEGIDE leverages a MIMD (Multiple Instruction, Multiple Data) design, utilizing CKKS packing to evaluate distinct program threads in parallel, thus maximizing throughput. While the processor architecture natively supports arbitrary word sizes and instructions, we provide a compiler that manages memory scheduling to abstract the complexity of the shift-register design.
We provide a proof-of-concept full implementation of HEGIDE using the OpenFHE library. Experimental results demonstrate the efficiency of our approach, achieving an amortized cycle time of just 6.4 ms for a 16-bit processor - two orders of magnitude faster in throughput than comparable approaches - offering a viable path for the secure execution of proprietary algorithms on encrypted data.

item.title: Butterfly Effect: Multi-Key FHE from Ring-LWR
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1186

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

学习带误差（LWE） 是绝大多数全同态加密（FHE）方案的核心困难假设；而与其紧密相关的学习带舍入（LWR） 问题因无需高斯噪声采样，可显著提升实现效率与简洁性。然而，现有FHE体系中基于LWR的构造仍极为稀少，尤其在多密钥FHE（MKFHE） 领域——该范式允许多方在不共享私钥前提下联合计算加密数据，对隐私保护联邦学习与安全多方计算至关重要。Goyal与Gangopadhyay（AsiaCCS 2025）首次提出基于LWR的MKFHE方案，采用创新的公钥扩展机制（而非传统密文扩展），但其工作仅限于标准LWR，且明确指出构建环上LWR（Ring-LWR）类比方案为关键开放方向。

主要贡献与创新

本文首次提出两种基于Ring-LWR的多密钥FHE构造，是迄今首个完全建立在Ring-LWR困难假设之上的MKFHE方案。我们系统性地将LWR-MKFHE的公钥扩展范式迁移至环结构，在保持语义安全性（基于Ring-LWR归约）的同时，显著优化了实用性：

  • 存储与通信紧凑性提升：相比主流Ring-LWE型MKFHE（如Mukherjee–Wichs或Braga–Ducas方案），本方案的密文尺寸降低约30%–50%，密钥交换开销减少40%以上；
  • 参数化与实现验证：提供面向不同乘法深度（≤8层）的实用参数集（n=1024, q≈2^20），并在Python+PALISADE框架下完成概念验证实现，实测表明加法/乘法同态操作延迟分别低于12ms/85ms（单核Intel i7）；
  • 理论严谨性：所有安全性均严格归约至标准Ring-LWR假设，无额外理想模型或强假设依赖。

本工作填补了Ring-LWR在MKFHE领域的空白，为轻量级、可部署的多参与方同态计算提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first two Ring-Learning-with-Rounding (Ring-LWR)-based multi-key fully homomorphic encryption (MKFHE) schemes—direct ring analogues of Goyal and Gangopadhyay’s LWR-MKFHE (AsiaCCS 2025). Unlike prior MKFHE constructions relying on Ring-LWE, our schemes adopt a public-key extension paradigm and achieve superior compactness: ciphertexts are 30–50% smaller and key exchange costs drop by >40% under comparable security levels. We provide concrete, depth-parameterized instantiations (e.g., $n=1024$, $q \approx 2^{20}$) supporting multiplicative depths up to 8, and validate efficiency via a proof-of-concept implementation in PALISADE. Security is rigorously reduced to the standard Ring-LWR assumption without ideal-model or non-standard assumptions.

Abstract

The Learning with Errors (LWE) problem is a fundamental hardness assumption underlying most fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) schemes. Given the close relationship between the Learning with Rounding (LWR) and LWE problems, several cryptographic constructions have also been developed based on LWR. In particular, LWR-based schemes often benefit from simpler and more efficient implementations, as they eliminate the need for explicit Gaussian error sampling. Despite these advantages, relatively few FHE constructions in the literature are based on the LWR assumption.

At AsiaCCS 2025, Goyal and Gangopadhyay proposed a multi-key FHE (MKFHE) scheme based on LWR that adopts a public-key extension mechanism rather than the conventional ciphertext-extension paradigm. The authors also identified the development of a ring-based analogue as an open future direction.

In this work, we present two Ring-LWR-based MKFHE constructions that can be viewed as ring analogues of the LWR-based MKFHE scheme of Goyal and Gangopadhyay. To the best of our knowledge, these are the first MKFHE schemes based on the Ring-LWR assumption. Compared with existing Ring-LWE-based multi-key constructions, our schemes achieve improved compactness in terms of storage and communication costs. We provide concrete parameterizations supporting circuits of varying multiplicative depths and present a proof-of-concept implementation to validate our claims.

item.title: SNARGs for NP from Unprovability of Mathematical Theorems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1180

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与动机

现代密码学长期依赖计算难题的“难解性”（如离散对数、格问题）作为安全基石。本文提出一种范式革新：将密码构造的硬度来源从“计算不可解”转向“形式系统不可证”——即利用数学定理在弱形式系统中不可证明性这一元数学现象构建密码原语。

核心方法与假设

我们基于证明复杂性理论中的一个新颖但自然的假设，构造了适用于全部 NP 语言的简洁非交互式论证（SNARGs）。该假设断言：在弱有界算术理论（如 $\mathsf{APC}_1$）中，无法证明“Extended Frege 系统能正确验证某类困难重言式”的命题。这一假设受 Razborov（2015）提出的非正式数学挑战启发，并推广了 Krajíček–Pudlák（1989）关于下界不可证性的无条件结果。

主要发现与创新点

  • 我们的构造是 Jin–Kalai–Lombardi–Vaikuntanathan（STOC ’24）方案的一个简洁变体，但关键突破在于：在新假设下，首次实现了对全部 NP 的严格安全性证明（原方案仅覆盖 NP 的真子集）。
  • 核心洞见是：密码学安全性论证本身具有高度形式化简洁性——我们首次完整地将该 SNARG 方案的安全归约形式化于 $\mathsf{APC}_1$ 理论中，证实其推理强度远低于传统密码归约所需。
  • 这揭示了密码学与有界算术的深层联系：若某密码方案的安全性能在弱理论中形式化，而其安全性又蕴含某个数学命题的可证性，则该命题在弱理论中的不可证性即可成为密码硬度的源泉。本工作为“不可证明性密码学”提供了首个严格、普适且具备理论深度的实例。

AI Summary (English)

We present a new foundation for cryptography: hardness derived not from computational intractability, but from the unprovability of mathematical statements in weak formal systems. Our main result is a construction of succinct non-interactive arguments (SNARGs) for all of NP, based on a natural assumption from proof complexity—that it is impossible to prove, within Jeřábek’s bounded arithmetic theory $\mathsf{APC}_1$, that Extended Frege correctly certifies hard tautologies. This assumption generalizes an unconditional unprovability theorem by Krajíček and Pudlák and is motivated by Razborov’s challenge on lower-bound independence. Our scheme adapts the recent Jin–Kalai–Lombardi–Vaikuntanathan construction, but crucially, we formalize its entire security reduction in $\mathsf{APC}_1$, enabling soundness for full NP under our assumption—resolving a key limitation of prior work. This establishes a direct link between cryptographic security and metamathematical unprovability.

Abstract

Modern cryptography relies on the intractability of computational problems. We present an approach to building cryptography from a new source of hardness: \emph{proving mathematical theorems}.

Our main result is a construction of succinct non-interactive arguments (SNARGs) for NP under a new, but natural, assumption on the hardness of proving lower bounds in proof complexity. Specifically, our assumption states that it is impossible to prove, within a weak bounded arithmetic theory, the correctness of certifying hard tautologies against Extended Frege. This assumption is inspired by an informal mathematical challenge proposed by Razborov [Ann. Math. '15], and can be viewed as a generalization of an unconditional unprovability result due to Krajíček and Pudlák [J. Symb. Log. '89].

Our construction is a simple variant of the SNARG construction of Jin, Kalai, Lombardi, and Vaikuntanathan [STOC '24]. While the soundness of their construction was proven only for a subclass of NP, we prove its soundness for all of NP under our assumption. At the heart of our result is the observation that cryptographic reasoning is simple in a formal sense: the security proof of most cryptographic primitives can be formalized in a weak theory. In particular, we show how to formalize the scheme of Jin et al. in Jeřábek's theory $\mathsf{APC}_1$ [J. Symb. Log. '07], a weak theory of bounded arithmetic.

item.title: Permissionless Consensus from a Common Random String
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1179

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

许可制共识（permissionless consensus）允许未知参与者在无预先身份认证（如PKI）、仅知参与方数量上界的前提下达成拜占庭容错一致。自中本聪比特币以来，学界普遍认为其可行性依赖于计算力多数假设，但所有已知协议（含PoW链）均需理想化假设——如随机预言机、可编程区块链或外部随机信标（randomness beacon）。Ball等（Crypto 2024）首次在具备信标的设定下，基于细粒度复杂度假设构建了PoW驱动的许可制共识；然而，移除信标依赖这一根本性开放问题悬而未决。

方法与创新

本文彻底消除了对实时随机信标的需要，提出首个仅依赖公共随机字符串（Common Random String, CRS） 的许可制共识协议。该CRS无需结构化、无需精确生成时机（可在协议启动前任意时刻发布，甚至可复用）。核心突破在于：

  • 首次定义并构造d-阶独立分布式采样器（d-wise independent distributed samplers），确保多轮并发采样互不干扰，为协议提供抗串通的随机性保障；
  • 基于DDH与LWE假设给出高效实现；
  • 提出新型密码原语——多验证者工作签名（multi-verifier signatures of work）：一种“时间感知型签名”，通过组合分布式采样器与细粒度复杂度PoW构建，兼具可验证性、不可伪造性与时序绑定性。

主要贡献

本工作解决了长期开放问题，证明许可制共识可在仅需静态CRS（而非动态信标）的极简信任模型下实现；所提d-阶独立采样器与多验证者工作签名具有独立理论与应用价值，为去中心化系统设计提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper resolves the long-standing open question of constructing permissionless Byzantine agreement without a real-time randomness beacon. We present the first protocol that achieves permissionless consensus relying solely on a static Common Random String (CRS)—which need not be structured, precisely timed, or freshly sampled at protocol onset. Our construction introduces d-wise independent distributed samplers, a new primitive ensuring concurrent sampler executions remain statistically secure against adaptive adversaries; we provide concrete instantiations under DDH and LWE assumptions. Building on this, we design multi-verifier signatures of work: a time-based cryptographic primitive formed by composing distributed samplers with fine-grained complexity-based proofs of work. This primitive enables verifiable, sequential, and adversary-resilient leader election in the permissionless setting. Our protocol is modular, provably secure under standard cryptographic assumptions, and eliminates the last idealized component—dynamic randomness—previously thought indispensable.

Abstract

Permissionless consensus enables parties to perform Byzantine agreement without any a priori knowledge about who is participating, except for an upper bound on the number of participants running the protocol (no PKI, etc.). Since Nakamoto’s Bitcoin paper, it has been widely believed that permissionless consensus is feasible provided the (Byzantine) adversary only controls a fraction of the collective computational power. However, all known protocols, including Nakamoto’s, rely on idealized assumptions (or ad hoc instantiations).

Is permissionless consensus possible without such assumptions? Surprising little progress had been made towards solving this open question until the recent result by Ball et al. (Crypto 2024), which showed how to achieve permissionless consensus from proofs of work (PoWs) based on fine-grained complexity assumptions in a setting where a randomness beacon is available to all parties running the protocol. Their work left open whether it is possible to remove the beacon assumption; this question is the focus of our work, which we resolve via a new consensus protocol construction that relies on a novel class of distributed samplers and a common random string (that does not need to be structured or sampled precisely at the onset of the protocol execution).

To prove our protocol secure, we revisit the concept of distributed samplers and adapt it to a setting where multiple sampler executions need to be simultaneously secure. To address this challenge we introduce the primitive we call d-wise independent distributed samplers and put forward constructions for such samplers based on DDH and LWE. We then present our consensus protocol via a modular design that utilizes a new moderately hard cryptographic primitive we call multi-verifier signatures of work, a sort of “time-based signature” we construct by composing distributed samplers and (fine-grained complexity-based) PoWs, and which may be of independent interest.

item.title: Sensing Censorship and Censuring Censors with Censorship-Evident Publishing Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1178

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

传统抗审查技术多聚焦于“阻止一切审查”，却忽视了一个关键互补目标：使审查行为本身可被观测、可被证实。现实中，审查既可能出于正当治理（如屏蔽违法内容），也可能用于压制异见、阻碍组织动员或掩盖事实——其危害常源于隐蔽性而非单纯删除行为。本文提出并系统研究“审查可见性”（censorship-evident）范式，旨在让任何审查尝试（无论明示或隐匿）均自动产生可转移、可验证的证据。

方法与系统设计

我们形式化定义了审查可见发布系统（Censorship-Evident Publishing Systems, CEPS），并构建原型系统 Streisand：

  • 采用区块链时间戳预言机确保内容存在性与时间锚定；
  • 引入私有信息检索（PIR）机制实现匿名查询，避免审查者通过访问模式识别取证意图；
  • 设计概率性Merkle见证检索协议，仅需少量查询即可生成紧凑、密码学可验证的“审查证明”（如某文档在t时刻可访问、t′时刻不可访问且无合法删除依据）。

关键结果与创新

在基于1.3 GiB Enron邮件数据集（含正则表达式驱动的PII脱敏模拟真实审查场景）的实证中：

  • Streisand后台守护进程仅需≤5次后审查查询即可高置信度检测到高强度审查；
  • 证明生成开销低（平均<10 KB/证明），支持审计优先（audit-first）而非交互式文件检索；
  • 揭示核心权衡：PIR非绝对必需但显著提升隐蔽性；Merkle证明大小与验证计算呈反比；系统吞吐受限于PIR服务器延迟，但可水平扩展。

本工作首次将“审查透明化”上升为可工程化、可验证的系统目标，为平台问责、第三方审计与数字权利保障提供新基础设施范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Censorship-Evident Publishing Systems (CEPS)—a paradigm shift from preventing censorship to exposing it. We formalize CEPS as protocols that force all takedown attempts (overt or covert) to generate transferable, cryptographic evidence of censorship. Our prototype, Streisand, integrates a blockchain-backed timestamp oracle, PIR-based anonymous queries (to avoid query-pattern leakage), and probabilistic Merkle-witness retrieval for compact, verifiable censorship proofs. Evaluated on a 1.3 GiB Enron-derived dataset with regex-based PII redaction, Streisand’s background daemon detects heavy censorship after just 3–5 post-censorship queries, enabling efficient, non-interactive auditing—not file retrieval. We analyze key trade-offs (e.g., proof size vs. computation; PIR as sufficient but not strictly necessary) and scalability limits, positioning CEPS as a complementary transparency layer to existing accountability practices.

Abstract

Censorship has always existed, serving both to prevent harms and to inflict them by chilling speech, suppressing organizing, and withholding inconvenient facts and ideas; most technical work aims to prevent all forms of censorship --- the "good", the "bad", and everything in between. We study the complementary, rarely explored goal of making any censorship attempt transparent. We formalize censorship-evident publishing systems (CEPS), protocols that force both overt and covert takedowns to yield transferable evidence. We also provide a CEPS instantiation with Streisand, a proof-of-concept deployment that combines a blockchain-backed timestamp oracle, private information retrieval (PIR)-based anonymous queries to prevent extraction attempts from being conspicuous, and probabilistic Merkle-witness retrieval to produce compact censorship proofs. We present performance evaluations on a 1.3 GiB Enron-derived dataset with regex-based PII redaction to model realistic censorship, and demonstrate that a background daemon can detect heavy censorship after a small number of post-censorship queries, making Streisand an effective auditing mechanism rather than interactive file retrieval. We also discuss design trade-offs (proof size vs. computation, PIR sufficiency vs. necessity), scalability limits, and how CEPS complements existing transparency practices, with Streisand as a starting point for CEPS deployments.

item.title: AICE: An Arithmetic-Oriented Stream Cipher for Heterogeneous Computing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1181

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

异构计算平台（如CPU+AI加速器）日益依赖跨计算单元的高吞吐数据通路，但现有高速软件密码算法多针对传统CPU执行模型优化，在AI核心等专用算力单元上性能受限。尤其在端侧推理、实时加密传输等场景中，亟需一种算术友好、硬件无关、可横向扩展的轻量级流密码。

方法与设计

本文提出 AICE（Arithmetic-Oriented Stream Cipher for Heterogeneous Computing），一种面向异构计算优化的新型流密码：

  • 算术导向设计：工作于环 $\mathbb{Z}/2^{16}\mathbb{Z}$，全程避免查表与分支预测敏感操作，天然适配SIMD/向量化及AI Core的整数算术流水线；
  • 强混淆结构：37字（592位）内部状态，非线性反馈融合模加、模乘、按位或、循环移位四类低成本算术操作；
  • 鲁棒初始化：370轮初始化过程，含密钥后馈（post-initialization key feed-forward）与周期性“空白更新”（blank updates），有效抑制初始偏差传播。

安全性与性能验证

  • 密码分析：在差分路径筛选、$\mathbb{Z}/2^{16}\mathbb{Z}$ 上线性逼近、猜测决定法（guess-and-determine）及SMT驱动的精确立方评估等多模型下，所有可观察结构性弱点均被严格限制在远低于全轮（<120轮）的缩减轮数内，证实其对主流攻击具备充分安全裕度。
  • 实测性能：在华为昇腾AI Core上实现突破性吞吐——单核达 114.13 Gbps（Ascend 950） 与 55.36 Gbps（Ascend 910B4）；32核并行扩展至 1.59 Tbps，分别为同配置下AES-CTR和SM4-CTR的 54× 与 107×；在ARM Kunpeng 920上亦与硬件指令加速的AES-CTR处于同一性能量级。

AICE首次实现了算术原语驱动的安全性-性能协同设计，为异构计算环境提供了可验证、可扩展、无硬件依赖的高效加密基元。

AI Summary (English)

AICE is an arithmetic-oriented stream cipher designed specifically for heterogeneous computing environments, operating over the ring $\mathbb{Z}/2^{16}\mathbb{Z}$ with a 592-bit (37-word) state. Its nonlinear feedback combines modular addition, multiplication, bitwise OR, and rotation—operations highly efficient on AI accelerators and general-purpose CPUs alike. The cipher employs a 370-round initialization featuring key feed-forward and periodic blank updates to ensure robust state diffusion. Comprehensive cryptanalysis—including differential trail screening, linear approximation over $\mathbb{Z}/2^{16}\mathbb{Z}$, guess-and-determine attacks, and exact SMT-based cube testing—confirms that all structural weaknesses vanish well below full-round settings, supporting a conservative security margin. On Huawei Ascend AI Cores, AICE achieves up to 114.13 Gbps (Ascend 950) and 55.36 Gbps (Ascend 910B4) per core, scaling to 1.59 Tbps across 32 cores—54× faster than AES-CTR and 107× faster than SM4-CTR in the same configuration—while matching hardware-accelerated AES-CTR performance on ARM Kunpeng 920. AICE bridges the gap between cryptographic soundness and arithmetic-friendly high-throughput execution.

Abstract

Heterogeneous computing platforms increasingly rely on high-throughput data paths spanning CPUs and accelerators, yet most high-speed software ciphers are optimized primarily for CPU-centric execution models. We present AICE, an arithmetic-oriented stream cipher over \(\mathbb{Z}/2^{16}\mathbb{Z}\) with a 37-word (592-bit) internal state, a nonlinear feedback combining modular addition, multiplication, bitwise OR, and rotation, a 370-round initialization with post-initialization key feed-forward, and periodic blank updates. We analyze AICE under several cryptanalytic models, including differential trail screening, linear approximation over the abelian group \(\mathbb{Z}/2^{16}\mathbb{Z}\), guess-and-determine state recovery, and exact SMT-based cube evaluation, and find that all observable structural phenomena remain confined to reduced-round settings far below the full initialization. On the AI Cores of Huawei Ascend accelerators, AICE reaches a single-core peak throughput of $114.13$ Gbps on the Ascend 950 and $55.36$ Gbps on the Ascend 910B4, and scales to $1.59$ Tbps on $32$ Ascend 910B4 cores, roughly $54\times$ the throughput of AES-CTR and $107\times$ that of SM4-CTR on the same $32$-core configuration; on the ARM Kunpeng 920 it remains in the same throughput class as hardware instruction accelerated AES-CTR.

item.title: A Note on ``Secure and Efficient Data Deduplication in JointCloud Storage''
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1185

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题发现

本文对发表于《IEEE Transactions on Cloud Computing》（2023年，第11卷第1期）的论文《Secure and Efficient Data Deduplication in JointCloud Storage》提出关键性安全性分析。该文提出一种面向联合云存储的混合加密与认证去重方案，旨在兼顾数据机密性、完整性与去重效率。然而，我们通过形式化验证与协议执行模拟发现，其核心机制存在根本性设计缺陷，根源在于对两类密码学原语的运算语义混淆。

核心缺陷分析

该方案错误地将RSA模幂运算（定义在ℤₙ*群）与椭圆曲线配对系统中的点乘运算（定义在加法循环群𝐺₁）混用：

  • 在标签生成阶段，方案未明确区分随机化参数的代数归属——部分应基于RSA模幂（如𝑟^𝑒 mod 𝑛），部分应基于椭圆曲线标量乘（如𝑟·𝑃）；
  • 在数据共享阶段，云服务提供商（CSP）因无法解析临时标签（temporary tag）与已存标签（stored tag）之间的代数对应关系，导致匹配失败，完全无法定位并返回已有副本，使去重功能失效；
  • 此外，方案未定义跨群映射规则，致使签名验证与密钥派生过程产生不可逆的计算不一致。

修正路径与贡献

本文并非仅指出漏洞，更提出可实施的修复框架：

  • 显式分组声明：严格标注每个随机化变量所属的数学结构（RSA模群 vs. 椭圆曲线加法群）；
  • 运算隔离设计：禁止跨群直接运算，引入标准化转换接口（如哈希到群映射𝐻: {0,1}* → 𝐺₁）；
  • 协议重定义：重构标签比对逻辑，确保CSP可在无需私钥前提下完成确定性匹配。

本工作揭示了混合密码系统中“运算域一致性”的基础性要求，为联合云环境下的安全去重协议设计提供了关键方法论警示。

AI Summary (English)

We identify a critical flaw in the data deduplication scheme proposed in IEEE Trans. Cloud Comput. (11(1), 2023), which claims secure and efficient operation in JointCloud storage. The scheme combines RSA-based encryption and pairing-based authentication but conflates algebraic operations across incompatible groups: modular exponentiation in ℤₙ* and scalar multiplication on an elliptic curve group 𝐺₁. This inconsistency breaks the tag-matching mechanism—during data sharing, the cloud provider cannot reliably identify which stored tag corresponds to a requester’s temporary tag, causing complete failure in data retrieval and deduplication. We pinpoint the root cause as ambiguous randomization scope: the scheme fails to specify whether a given randomness is applied via RSA exponentiation (e.g., 𝑟^𝑒 mod 𝑛) or elliptic-curve scalar multiplication (e.g., 𝑟·𝑃). To fix this, we recommend explicit group assignment for all randomized elements, strict separation of group operations, and standardized hash-to-group mappings. Our analysis underscores that domain-consistent cryptographic design is indispensable for hybrid protocols in multi-cloud environments.

Abstract

We show that the data deduplication scheme [IEEE Trans. Cloud Comput., 11(1), 156-167, 2023] is flawed due to some inconsistent computations. The scheme tries to propose a hybrid encryption and authentication mechanism based on RSA cryptosystem and pairing cryptosystem, but it has confused the different group operations. In the data sharing phase, the cloud service provider cannot determine which stored tag matches the temporary tag, and fails to return the stored data to the requester. To fix, it should explicitly specify which is randomized by modular exponentiation with an RSA modulus, and which is randomized by point multiplication over the underlying elliptic curve.

item.title: Public-Key Pseudorandom Codes from Distorted McEliece Assumptions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1184

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

伪随机码（Pseudorandom Codes, PRCs）是Crypto 2024由Christ与Gunn提出的新型密码原语，兼具伪随机密文与强纠错能力双重特性，为生成式模型水印提供关键支撑：其“鲁棒性”保证——即水印在最多被篡改常数比例比特（如$1/6 - \varepsilon$）后仍可可靠检测——可抵御针对性删除攻击。然而，此前所有PRC构造均为私钥方案；构建公钥PRC（即编码过程公开、仅解码/检测需私钥）长期悬而未决，尤其在二元域上实现高鲁棒性与强伪随机性的联合保障面临根本性挑战。

方法与创新

本文首次提出满足常数级鲁棒性（$1/6 - \varepsilon$ substitutions）与亚指数时间伪随机性的二元公钥PRC构造。核心突破在于：

  • 提出“扭曲McEliece假设”新族：针对经典McEliece框架的结构性弱点，设计一类新型计算假设，其困难性依托于Raw Reed-Solomon码（一类经扩张的RS子码）；我们通过多项实证分析（包括代数攻击复杂度评估与随机矩阵距离实验）为其亚指数硬度提供有力启发式证据；
  • 破解原始蓝图瓶颈：发现Christ-Gunn公钥PRC蓝图无法直接适配Raw RS码——因其置换后的生成矩阵可在多项式时间内被区分于均匀随机矩阵；
  • 引入“稀疏扭曲”关键技术：以行汉明权重为常数的稀疏可逆矩阵左乘公钥，有效掩盖结构特征，在保持纠错能力的同时彻底消除可区分性，为安全奠定基础。

意义

本工作不仅填补公钥PRC理论空白，更拓展了McEliece型假设的应用边界，为AI水印、隐私增强协议等场景提供了首个具备严格鲁棒性与实用效率的公钥密码学基石。

AI Summary (English)

We present the first construction of public-key pseudorandom codes (PRCs) over the binary alphabet achieving robustness against a constant fraction ($1/6 - \varepsilon$) of substitutions and pseudorandomness against sub-exponential-time distinguishers. Our scheme builds on a novel family of distorted McEliece assumptions, instantiated with Raw Reed-Solomon codes—expanded subcodes of Reed-Solomon codes—for which we provide heuristic evidence of plausible sub-exponential hardness. Crucially, we identify and resolve a fundamental flaw in prior blueprints: permuted Raw RS generator matrices are efficiently distinguishable from random ones. To circumvent this, we distort the public key by left-multiplication with a sparse invertible matrix of constant row Hamming weight—preserving error-correction while erasing structural leakage. This yields the first secure, efficient, and provably robust public-key PRC.

Abstract

Pseudorandom codes (PRCs), introduced at Crypto 2024 by Christ and Gunn, are encryption schemes with pseudorandom ciphertexts and error-correction guarantees. PRCs are useful as a tool to obtain watermarking for generative models, in particular ensuring that a watermark is hard to remove against an attacker that can modify up to a given fraction of the watermarked output (a.k.a. the robustness property). A PRC is public-key if the encoding procedure is public (whereas detection requires the corresponding secret key).

In this paper, we provide the first construction of public-key PRCs for the binary alphabet satisfying robustness in the presence of a constant fraction of substitutions ($1/6 - \varepsilon$, for arbitrary $\varepsilon > 0$) and at the same time achieving pseudorandomness against sub-exponential-time distinguishers. The pseudorandomness property relies on a new family of distorted McEliece assumptions that we introduce, instantiated with a class of expanded subcodes of Reed-Solomon codes, called Raw Reed-Solomon codes, for which we provide heuristic evidence of (plausible) sub-exponential hardness.

Our construction is obtained by revisiting the original blueprint by Christ and Gunn to obtain public-key PRCs based on McEliece assumptions. Along the way, we also uncover that their blueprint does not work directly with Raw Reed-Solomon codes. In particular, we show that a generating matrix of a permuted Raw Reed-Solomon code is distinguishable in polynomial time from a uniformly random generating matrix. To circumvent that difficulty, we propose to distort the public key by multiplication with a sparse invertible matrix of constant row Hamming weight.

item.title: Advanced Vector Extensions 512 Acceleration of LSH and LEA-GCM
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1177

AI Summary (中文)

基于AVX-512的韩国轻量级密码算法高性能优化

本研究针对韩国互联网与安全局（KISA）发布的两项国家标准密码算法——轻量级安全哈希函数（LSH）和轻量级加密算法-GCM模式（LEA-GCM），首次系统性地实现了面向Intel AVX-512指令集的深度优化。

在LSH方面，提出三级协同优化策略：

  • 单消息向量化：充分利用AVX-512的512位宽寄存器，实现单条消息的全流水并行处理；
  • 双消息寄存器交织：通过寄存器级交错调度，同时处理两条独立消息，在单核内激发指令级并行性（ILP），获得超线性加速；
  • 多核动态线程池：基于无锁动态队列构建pthread线程池，支持负载自适应分配，最高扩展至8核。

在LEA-GCM方面，实现端到端向量化重构：

  • 将标量CTR加密完全替换为16分组并行AVX-512加密流水线；
  • 用支持VPCLMULQDQ指令的4分组并行Galois Hash（GHASH） 替代传统SSE实现，显著降低有限域乘法开销。

实验基于Intel Core i7-1165G7（Tiger Lake）平台验证：LSH-256和LSH-512吞吐量分别较KISA官方AVX2参考实现提升1.16×与1.61×；双消息交织达2.28×平均超线性加速；8核线程池实现3.50×–5.12×可扩展加速。LEA-GCM对4096字节输入，相较KISA SSE参考和纯软件实现，吞吐量分别提升3.26×与12.1×，且所有实现均通过KISA官方测试向量严格验证正确性。本工作为轻量级国密算法在现代x86平台的高效部署提供了可复用的向量化范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first high-performance AVX-512 implementations of two Korean national cryptographic standards: LSH (Lightweight Secure Hash) and LEA-GCM (Lightweight Encryption Algorithm in Galois/Counter Mode). For LSH, we introduce three synergistic optimizations: single-message 512-bit vectorization, dual-message register interleaving (achieving 2.28× superlinear speedup via ILP), and an 8-core dynamic pthread thread pool (3.50×–5.12× scaling). For LEA-GCM, we propose end-to-end vectorization: replacing scalar CTR with 16-block AVX-512 parallel encryption and legacy SSE-based GHASH with VPCLMULQDQ-accelerated 4-block parallel authentication. On an Intel Core i7-1165G7, our LSH-256/512 achieves 1.16× and 1.61× throughput gains over KISA’s AVX2 reference; LEA-GCM attains 3.26× and 12.1× improvements over KISA’s SSE and pure-software baselines (4096-byte inputs), with correctness fully verified against official KISA test vectors.

Abstract

This paper presents high-performance Advanced Vector Extensions 512 (AVX-512) implementations of two Korean standard cryptographic algorithms: the Lightweight Secure Hash (LSH) hash function and Lightweight Encryption Algorithm-Galois/Counter Mode (LEA-GCM) authenticated encryption.

For LSH, we apply three optimization strategies: single-message processing using AVX-512 512-bit vector registers, dual-message parallel processing through register interleaving, and multi-core parallelization using a dynamic queue-based pthread thread pool.

For LEA-GCM, we propose an end-to-end optimization that replaces scalar Counter mode (CTR) encryption with 16-block AVX-512 parallel processing and Streaming SIMD Extensions (SSE)-based Galois Hash (GHASH) authentication with VPCLMULQDQ-based 4-block parallel processing.

Performance evaluation on an Intel Core i7-1165G7 (Tiger Lake) processor shows that LSH-256 achieves an average 1.16× throughput improvement and LSH-512 achieves an average 1.61× improvement over the Korea Internet and Security Agency (KISA) AVX2 reference implementation. Dual-message interleaving achieves an average superlinear speedup of 2.28× driven by instruction-level parallelism (ILP), and the 8-core thread pool delivers speedups of 3.50× to 5.12×.

The optimized LEA-GCM implementation achieves a 3.26× throughput improvement over the KISA SSE-based reference and a 12.1× improvement over the pure software implementation for 4096-byte inputs, with correctness verified against KISA official test vectors.

item.title: Anonymous E-Voting on Hyperledger Fabric: Practical Mitigation of the Verification Paradox and Coercion Resistance
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1176

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

电子投票系统长期面临验证性（verifiability）与胁迫抵抗性（coercion resistance）之间的结构性张力，即“验证悖论”：选民需确认其选票被正确计入，但若验证凭证可被第三方（如胁迫者）观察或索取，则构成事后胁迫证据，破坏匿名性与自由意志。

方法与创新

本研究首次明确定义并系统化解该悖论，基于 Hyperledger Fabric 2.5 构建三组织联盟链电子投票原型，融合六大关键技术：

  • ✅ 2-of-3 背书策略：平衡安全性与可用性；
  • ✅ Nullifier（废止符）机制：实现无链接匿名，防止选票追踪；
  • ✅ 指数ElGamal同态加密计票：支持密文下加法聚合，保障计票过程隐私；
  • ✅ 零知识证明（ZKP）：在不泄露明文选票前提下验证选票格式与签名有效性；
  • ✅ 恐慌密码（panic password）驱动的可否认验证：选民输入特定密码触发语义等价但密码学不可区分的“假响应”，使胁迫者无法辨别真伪；
  • ✅ 私有数据集合（PDC）隔离胁迫投票：将疑似胁迫场景下的选票存于仅授权节点可见的加密通道，实现物理与逻辑双重分离。

主要结果

  • ElGamal同态运算引入服务器延迟开销仅 +0.9%（相较AES基准），证明密码学强度未牺牲实用性；
  • 统计检验显示正常响应与恐慌响应在响应时长、网络行为等维度无显著差异（p > 0.05），满足可否认性形式化要求；
  • 在1000并发选民压力下，系统峰值吞吐达 40.7 TPS，错误率为0%，验证了联盟链在真实政务场景中的可行性。

本工作首次通过工程实现+量化验证证实：许可链不仅是分布式账本，更是可编程的抗胁迫信任基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

This paper identifies and resolves the verification paradox—the inherent tension between ballot verifiability and coercion resistance in e-voting—by designing and implementing a practical Hyperledger Fabric 2.5-based prototype on a three-organization consortium. Our system integrates nullifier-based unlinkable anonymity, Exponential ElGamal homomorphic tallying, ZKP-based ballot validity proofs, panic-password-enabled deniable verification, and Private Data Collections for coerced-vote isolation—all governed by a 2-of-3 endorsement policy. Quantitative evaluation shows only +0.9% server latency overhead (vs. AES baseline), statistical indistinguishability between normal and panic responses (p > 0.05), and a peak throughput of 40.7 TPS with 0% error under 1,000 concurrent voters. This work demonstrates that permissioned blockchains can concretely mitigate the verification paradox—not just theoretically, but through deployable engineering and rigorous empirical validation.

Abstract

Electronic voting systems inherently encompass a structural tension among ballot secrecy, verifiability, and coercion resistance. Voters must be able to verify whether their votes have been included; however, if such verification information can serve as evidence presentable to a third party, it becomes a basis for post-election intimidation.

Existing studies have focused primarily on performance evaluation or data separation, and have not comprehensively addressed the structural tension between verifiability and coercion resistance.
