This study defines this tension as the verification paradox and designs and implements an electronic voting prototype on a three-organization consortium based on Hyperledger Fabric 2.5, combining a 2-of-3 endorsement policy, nullifier-based anonymity, Exponential ElGamal homomorphic tallying, zero-knowledge proof (ZKP)-based ballot validity verification, panic password-based deniable verification, and Private Data Collection (PDC)-based coerced vote separation.

Quantitative evaluation results confirm a server latency overhead of +0.9% for ElGamal relative to the AES performance baseline, statistical indistinguishability between normal and panic responses (p > 0.05), and a peak throughput of approximately 40.7 TPS (with an error rate of 0%) under 1,000 concurrent voters.

This study demonstrates that permissioned blockchains can provide practical mitigation of the verification paradox through implementation and quantitative evaluation.

item.title: AI-based KCMVP Pre-certification System: A Hybrid Model of Rule-based Detection and LLM Semantic Analysis
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1175

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

韩国密码模块验证计划（KCMVP）是面向政府及公共机构部署的密码模块实施安全合规性认证的国家级体系。当前认证流程平均耗时约18个月，频繁的补充材料要求与反复测试显著推高成本、延误项目交付。

方法：三级漏斗式AI预认证框架

我们提出AI驱动的KCMVP预认证系统，融合规则确定性检测（L1）、RAG增强型指南证据检索（L2）与大语言模型语义决策（L3），构建渐进式降噪漏斗结构：

  • L1层（规则引擎）：基于170+条YAML格式检查规则，覆盖缺失项、正则匹配、语义约束、AST结构四类模式，实现零误报（zero-FN）的确定性违规识别；
  • L2层（RAG检索）：针对L1输出的每项违规，通过多阶段检索从KCMVP官方指南中精准关联上下文证据，提升可解释性；
  • L3层（LLM决策）：采用Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite模型，结合L2检索证据进行上下文感知推理，对L1产生的假阳性（FP）候选集进行最终裁决。

主要结果与创新

  • 在128个源自KISA LEA代码的真实标注样本上：100%召回率（检出全部缺陷），L3成功剔除46个FP中的9个（19.6% FP缓解率），且零漏判（0 FN），验证漏斗结构的逐级净化效能；
  • 对某已认证商用密码模块（~14.5 KLOC）盲测显示：缺陷密度仅0.58例/千行代码，印证系统在真实工程场景中的低干扰性与实用性；
  • 首次将规则引擎—RAG—轻量级LLM三阶协同范式应用于密码合规自动化预审，兼顾确定性、可追溯性与语义泛化能力。

AI Summary (English)

The Korean Cryptographic Module Validation Program (KCMVP) faces severe efficiency bottlenecks—averaging 18 months per certification—with high costs and delays driven by iterative supplement requests and retesting. To address this, we propose an AI-based pre-certification system structured as a three-stage funnel: (1) L1 applies >170 deterministic YAML rules across four pattern types (missing, regex, semantic, AST) for zero-FN violation detection; (2) L2 employs multi-stage RAG to retrieve and attach official KCMVP guideline evidence to each violation; (3) L3 leverages Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite to make context-aware decisions on L1’s false-positive candidates. Evaluated on 128 ground-truth cases from KISA LEA code, the system achieves 100% recall, and L3 reduces false positives by 19.6% (9/46) with zero false negatives, confirming the funnel’s refinement efficacy. Blind testing on a certified commercial module (~14.5 KLOC) yields only 0.58 violations per 1,000 LOC—demonstrating practical viability. This work pioneers a hybrid rule–RAG–LLM pipeline for cryptographic compliance automation, balancing determinism, auditability, and semantic reasoning.

Abstract

The Korean Cryptographic Module Validation Program (KCMVP) is a national certification system that verifies the security and conformity of cryptographic modules deployed in government and public institutions. The current process typically takes about one and a half years, during which frequent supplement requests and the resulting retesting cycles substantially raise costs and delay schedules.

To address this, we propose an AI-based pre-certification framework that combines rule-based deterministic detection (L1), RAG-based guideline-evidence retrieval (L2), and LLM-based final decision (L3) into a funnel-shaped pipeline that progressively reduces false positives.

L1 applies more than 170 YAML inspection rules across four pattern types (missing, regex, semantic, ast) to perform deterministic detection. L2 retrieves and attaches KCMVP guideline evidence to each violation through multi-stage RAG search, and L3 employs Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite to make context-aware decisions on false-positive candidates.

In an initial evaluation on 128 Ground Truth cases derived from the KISA LEA code, the system detected all 128 cases, achieving 100% recall, while L3 correctly removed 9 of 46 FP candidates (19.6%) without inducing any false negatives (FN), confirming the stepwise refinement effect of the funnel structure.

A blind verification on a certified commercial cryptographic module (~14.5 KLOC) yielded a low detection frequency of 0.58 cases per 1,000 lines of code, supporting the system’s practicality in real environments.

item.title: A Layered Risk Scoring Model for TLS Connections Against Quantum Threats
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1174

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

量子计算的快速发展正严重威胁当前TLS协议中广泛使用的公钥密码算法（如RSA、ECDHE）。Harvest Now, Decrypt Later（HNDL）攻击使得攻击者可在今日截获加密流量，待未来量子计算机成熟后解密——这意味着即使量子硬件尚未实用化，长期敏感数据已处于实质性风险之中。然而，目前缺乏一种可即时、细粒度、可部署的风险量化方法，用以评估单个TLS连接在量子威胁下的真实脆弱性。

方法与模型创新

本文提出首个分层式TLS量子风险评分模型（0–100分制），将量子威胁解耦为五个正交维度：

  • L1：TLS协议暴露层（如是否启用TLS 1.2+、是否禁用不安全扩展）；
  • L2a：传统公钥脆弱层（RSA/ECC密钥长度、密钥交换机制强度）；
  • L2b：AES-128 Grover削弱层（基于Grover算法对对称密钥的二次加速攻击影响）；
  • L2c：PQC Level-1脆弱层（是否采用NIST首批标准化后量子密码算法，如CRYSTALS-Kyber）；
  • L3：证书过期紧迫层（剩余有效期越短，重签PQC证书窗口越小，风险越高）。

模型引入全局HNDL乘数M（取值反映数据保密期要求，如M=1.50对应高敏数据需保障15年以上机密性），实现风险动态加权。

主要发现与意义

基于对502条真实TLS会话（覆盖金融、政务、医疗、教育四大行业）的实证分析（设M=1.50），结果显示：国内平均风险评分为16.0分，显著高于全球均值11.9分（+34%）；其根源在于国内77.4%的连接仍依赖易受Shor算法攻击的遗留密钥交换（全球仅53.5%），而PQC采用率仅为22.6%（全球达46.5%）。该量化证据首次系统揭示了我国网络基础设施在量子迁移进程中的结构性滞后，为监管制定、厂商优先级排序与迁移路线图提供了可审计、可比较的决策依据。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a novel layered risk scoring model (0–100 scale) to quantify quantum threat exposure of individual TLS connections—addressing the urgent gap in operational quantum-risk assessment amid Harvest Now, Decrypt Later (HNDL) threats. The model decomposes risk into five orthogonal layers: TLS protocol exposure (L1), legacy public-key vulnerability (L2a), AES-128 Grover weakening (L2b), PQC Level-1 adoption status (L2c), and certificate expiration urgency (L3), all scaled by an HNDL global multiplier M reflecting data confidentiality retention requirements. Applied to 502 real-world TLS sessions across four sectors (M = 1.50), domestic average risk (16.0) is 34% higher than the global average (11.9), driven by markedly lower PQC adoption (22.6% vs. 46.5%) and significantly higher legacy key exchange usage (77.4% vs. 53.5%). These results provide the first empirical, granular confirmation of elevated quantum risk in domestic infrastructure, underscoring the critical need for accelerated, metrics-driven PQC transition.

Abstract

The advancement of quantum computing threatens public-key cryptographic algorithms used in TLS connections, such as RSA and ECDHE. The Harvest Now, Decrypt Later (HNDL) attack exposes long-term confidential data to risk even before quantum computers are practically realized, yet a systematic methodology for immediately quantifying the quantum vulnerability of individual TLS connections remains absent.

This paper proposes a layered risk scoring model that quantifies the quantum threat exposure of TLS connections on a 0–100 scale. The model decomposes risk into five independent layers—TLS protocol exposure (L1), legacy public-key vulnerability (L2a), AES-128 Grover weakening (L2b), PQC Level-1 vulnerability (L2c), and certificate expiration urgency (L3)—and incorporates an HNDL global multiplier M that reflects the confidentiality retention period of transmitted data.

Applied to 502 real-world TLS sessions across four industry sectors with M = 1.50, the domestic average risk score (16.0) is approximately 34% higher than the global average (11.9), with domestic legacy key exchange usage at 77.4% versus 53.5% globally and PQC adoption at 22.6% versus 46.5%.

These results quantitatively confirm the elevated quantum risk of domestic network infrastructure, underscoring the urgency of PQC transition.

item.title: Automated Phased Hybrid PQC-TLS Migration via DevSecOps Pipeline
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1173

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

“现在收割、未来解密（HNDL）”攻击通过预先截获并存储TLS加密流量，待量子计算机成熟后批量破解，对现有公钥基础设施构成长达数十年的生存性威胁。NIST后量子密码（PQC）标准发布后，组织亟需实施分阶段TLS迁移——从经典ECC→混合PQC→高级混合PQC。然而，当前实践存在三大瓶颈：配置错误发现滞后、密码资产缺乏全生命周期追踪、自动化响应能力薄弱，导致迁移过程风险高、验证盲区大。

方法创新

本文提出首个面向PQC-TLS迁移的11步DevSecOps流水线，以策略即代码（Policy as Code）为内核，实现端到端自动化：

  • ✅ 三阶段TLS演进引擎：自动编排Classical ECC（S1）、Hybrid PQC（S2）、Advanced Hybrid PQC（S3）的渐进式部署；
  • ✅ 深度集成工具链：基于OQS-Nginx定制构建、SBOM差异分析（CBOM Diff）、多维CI矩阵回归检测、动态安全扫描；
  • ✅ 语义级防御闭环：突破静态分析局限，将AST校验与运行时语义执行验证强制耦合，捕获LLM生成代码中的签名/参数幻觉等深层语义缺陷。

关键实证结果

  • 在6类PQC工具链典型陷阱测试中：传统SCA/SAST/SBOM预检0/6；定制构建前置拦截5/6；矩阵CI在运行时实现6/6全检出；
  • LLM辅助代码迁移80次实验（55个有效响应）显示：即使启用静态AST校验，仍有24次失败（43.6%）仅在运行时暴露——确证语义执行验证不可替代。

本工作首次将DevSecOps范式系统化延伸至PQC迁移领域，为抗量子演进提供可审计、可回滚、可量化的工程化路径。

AI Summary (English)

The “Harvest Now, Decrypt Later” (HNDL) threat necessitates urgent, phased migration of TLS to NIST-standardized post-quantum cryptography (PQC). This paper introduces an 11-step DevSecOps pipeline, grounded in Policy as Code, that automates validation and deployment across three TLS migration stages: Classical ECC → Hybrid PQC → Advanced Hybrid PQC. It integrates OQS-Nginx custom builds, security scanning, CBOM Diff, and matrix-based CI regression detection to enforce cryptographic asset traceability and rapid response. Evaluation shows the pipeline pre-prevents 5/6 known PQC toolchain pitfalls via custom builds and detects all 6/6 at runtime—outperforming standard SCA/SAST/SBOM tools (0/6 detected). Crucially, in 80 LLM-assisted migration trials (55 valid responses), 24 failures (43.6%) caused by signature/argument hallucination evaded static AST validation but were caught only at runtime, quantitatively proving that semantic execution validation is indispensable in multi-layered PQC defense. The pipeline delivers auditable, rollback-safe, and measurable quantum resilience.

Abstract

The “Harvest Now, Decrypt Later (HNDL)” attack—which intercepts and stores encrypted communications before quantum computers become practical and decrypts them afterward—poses a long-term threat to TLS communication channels. Following the completion of NIST PQC standardization, organizations must pursue a phased migration of their TLS layers; however, existing approaches are limited by late detection of configuration errors, absence of cryptographic asset tracking, and insufficient automated response.

This paper proposes an 11-Step DevSecOps pipeline grounded in Policy as Code. The pipeline automatically validates and deploys a 3-Stage TLS migration—Classical ECC (Stage 1), Hybrid PQC (Stage 2), and Advanced Hybrid PQC (Stage 3)—through 11 steps per execution, integrating OQS-Nginx custom builds, security scanning, CBOM Diff, and matrix CI regression detection.

In our evaluation against six PQC toolchain pitfalls, standard SCA/SAST/SBOM pre-detected 0/6, whereas custom builds pre-prevented 5/6 and matrix CI detected 6/6 at runtime. In an evaluation of LLM-based code migration across 80 trials (55 responses received), even with static AST validation applied, 24 failures due to signature/argument hallucination (43.6% of response trials) were only caught at runtime, quantitatively confirming that semantic execution validation is an essential component of multi-layered defense.
